quick_solutions: Proton pump inhibitors (omeprazole, pantoprazole) are highly effective for managing reflux symptoms caused by hiatal hernia
Antacids provide on-demand relief for occasional breakthrough symptoms
Maintain healthy body weight — obesity significantly increases intra-abdominal pressure and hernia symptoms
Surgical repair (laparoscopic Nissen fundoplication) is recommended for large symptomatic hernias or those with complications
hiatal hernia.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for sudden severe chest or upper abdominal pain, inability to swallow, vomiting blood, or if the hernia becomes incarcerated (trapped) — a rare but serious complication requiring urgent surgery.

immediate_advice: During an acute vertigo attack, lie still in a safe position on the floor or bed to prevent falls
Fix your gaze on a stationary object — this activates the vestibulo-ocular reflex and can reduce vertigo intensity
Avoid driving or operating machinery during and for 24 hours after an acute attack
quick_solutions: Reduce dietary sodium intake to less than 1.5g per day — this is the single most consistently effective lifestyle intervention
Betahistine (if available) taken regularly reduces the frequency of vertigo attacks in many patients
Vestibular rehabilitation physiotherapy reduces chronic imbalance and improves compensation
Intratympanic gentamicin or steroid injections, or endolymphatic sac surgery, are options for refractory cases
meniere disease.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent evaluation for sudden severe hearing loss, new onset severe vertigo lasting more than 24 hours, tinnitus in one ear, or a drop attack (sudden fall without warning without loss of consciousness).

immediate_advice: Take warm sitz baths (sitting in 5–8cm of warm water) for 10–15 minutes after each bowel motion to relax the sphincter and promote healing
Apply topical anesthetic (lidocaine gel) before bowel movements to reduce pain if severe
Increase dietary fiber intake immediately to soften stools and reduce the trauma of defecation
quick_solutions: Topical glyceryl trinitrate (GTN) ointment or calcium channel blocker cream (diltiazem, nifedipine) applied twice daily are first-line treatments that relax the internal sphincter
Stool softeners (lactulose, docusate sodium) or bulk-forming laxatives (psyllium) reduce straining
Ensure adequate hydration (at least 2 liters of water daily) to maintain soft stools
Botulinum toxin injection or lateral internal sphincterotomy (surgical) are options for fissures not healing after 8 weeks of medical treatment
anal fissure.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor if pain is severe and not improving after 2 weeks of treatment, if there is significant bleeding with defecation, or if you notice a skin tag, discharge, or induration which may indicate a chronic fissure, Crohn's disease, or other pathology.

immediate_advice: For anaphylaxis (severe allergic reaction with difficulty breathing, swelling of throat, or collapse): administer epinephrine (adrenaline) auto-injector if available and call emergency services immediately
Remove or avoid the identified allergen immediately
Take a non-sedating antihistamine (cetirizine, loratadine) for mild to moderate allergic reactions
quick_solutions: Identify and consistently avoid confirmed allergens through allergy testing (skin prick test or specific IgE blood test)
Carry a prescribed epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) at all times if you have a history of anaphylaxis
Allergen immunotherapy (allergy shots or sublingual drops) can desensitize and reduce severity over 3–5 years
Wear a medical alert bracelet identifying your allergy if severe reactions have occurred previously
allergy.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for throat swelling, difficulty breathing, sudden drop in blood pressure, or loss of consciousness — these indicate anaphylaxis which is rapidly fatal without epinephrine. See an allergist for all moderate-to-severe allergies for formal testing and management.

immediate_advice: Keep the ear dry — use a cotton wool ball coated in petroleum jelly to protect the ear during showering or hair washing
Never insert objects into the ear canal as this can worsen perforation or introduce infection
Avoid swimming and diving until cleared by an ENT specialist
quick_solutions: Prescribed antibiotic ear drops (ciprofloxacin or combination antibiotic-steroid drops) treat acute-on-chronic infections
Aural toilet (microsuction or dry mopping by a clinician) removes discharge and improves healing
Tympanoplasty (surgical repair of the eardrum) and mastoidectomy are definitive treatments for persistent disease
Audiological assessment and hearing aid fitting may be required if significant conductive hearing loss is present
chronic otitis media.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent ENT care for sudden hearing loss, severe ear pain, facial weakness, vertigo, or severe headache in the context of known chronic ear disease — these may indicate dangerous intracranial extension (mastoiditis, meningitis, brain abscess).

immediate_advice: Elevate the affected limb above heart level when resting to promote lymphatic drainage
Wear prescribed compression garments consistently during waking hours
Protect the affected limb from cuts, burns, and infections — even minor skin breaks can cause severe cellulitis
quick_solutions: Complete Decongestive Therapy (CDT) performed by a certified lymphedema therapist is the gold standard treatment
Manual Lymphatic Drainage (MLD) massage performed by a trained therapist reduces limb volume
Skin care with regular moisturizing using pH-neutral emollients prevents infection and tissue breakdown
Maintain healthy body weight as obesity significantly worsens lymphedema and reduces treatment effectiveness
lymphedema.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent care for sudden worsening of swelling, redness, warmth, and tenderness of the limb with fever (acute cellulitis requiring IV antibiotics), or any rapid change in limb appearance.

immediate_advice: Administer short-acting bronchodilator (salbutamol) inhaler immediately — 4–8 puffs via spacer device
Sit upright and try to remain calm; anxiety worsens bronchospasm
Call emergency services if no improvement after two doses of bronchodilator 20 minutes apart
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care for severe bronchospasm not responding to bronchodilators
Identify and remove the trigger: allergen, cold air, exercise, smoke, or respiratory infection
Oral corticosteroids (prednisolone) are prescribed to reduce airway inflammation in moderate-to-severe episodes
Ipratropium bromide (anticholinergic) added to salbutamol provides greater bronchodilation in emergency settings
acute bronchospasm.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for severe breathlessness at rest, inability to complete sentences, oxygen saturation below 94%, silent chest on examination, or failure to respond to initial bronchodilator treatment.

immediate_advice: Seek emergency ophthalmological care immediately — acute angle-closure glaucoma can cause permanent blindness within hours
Do not take any decongestant medications, anticholinergics, or antihistamines as these can worsen angle closure
Lie on your back (supine) if possible — gravity may partially open the drainage angle
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — IV acetazolamide, topical timolol, pilocarpine, and IV hyperosmotic agents are initiated in hospital to lower intraocular pressure urgently
Laser peripheral iridotomy is the definitive treatment and is performed as soon as the eye is sufficiently quiet
The fellow (unaffected) eye should also receive prophylactic laser iridotomy as it is at high risk of the same event
Lifelong monitoring of intraocular pressure is required after an acute attack
acute glaucoma.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for sudden severe eye pain, halos around lights, red eye, fixed mid-dilated pupil, nausea, vomiting, and blurred vision — this is an ocular emergency.

immediate_advice: Continue gentle movement — bed rest beyond 1–2 days worsens sciatica and delays recovery
Apply heat to the lower back and buttock area for 15–20 minutes to relax muscle spasm
Avoid sitting on hard surfaces or in low seats which increase sciatic nerve compression
quick_solutions: NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen) are first-line for pain management
Physiotherapy focusing on neural mobilization, lumbar stabilization exercises, and postural education is highly effective
Epidural corticosteroid injection provides significant short-term pain relief for severe or refractory cases
Surgical discectomy is considered if neurological deficits progress, pain is intractable after 6 weeks, or cauda equina syndrome develops
sciatica.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for new bladder or bowel dysfunction, saddle anesthesia (numbness in the groin or inner thighs), or rapidly progressive leg weakness — these indicate cauda equina syndrome requiring emergency surgery.

immediate_advice: Begin pelvic floor muscle exercises (Kegel exercises) immediately — contract the pelvic floor for 8–10 seconds, relax, repeat 10 times, 3 sets daily
Reduce or eliminate caffeine and alcohol which increase urinary urgency and leakage
Use absorbent pads as a short-term measure while starting treatment
quick_solutions: Supervised pelvic floor physiotherapy with a women's health or continence physiotherapist is the most effective first-line treatment
Maintain healthy body weight — weight loss of even 5–10% significantly reduces incontinence episodes
Avoid constipation and straining as this weakens pelvic floor muscles over time
Surgical options (mid-urethral sling, colposuspension) are highly effective for cases not responding to conservative treatment
stress incontinence.when_to_seek_help: Consult a continence specialist or gynecologist if leakage is affecting quality of life, if pelvic floor exercises have not improved symptoms after 3 months of consistent practice, or if associated with pelvic organ prolapse.

immediate_advice: Most simple kidney cysts require no immediate action — they are benign and commonly found incidentally on imaging
Stay well hydrated to support overall kidney health
Avoid NSAIDs regularly as they reduce kidney blood flow, particularly important when kidney cysts are present
quick_solutions: Simple benign cysts (Bosniak I and II) require only surveillance ultrasound every 1–3 years — no treatment is needed
Maintain blood pressure within normal range as hypertension can accelerate any underlying kidney disease
Follow up with your doctor if cysts are large (greater than 5cm), increasing in size, or causing symptoms
Complex cysts (Bosniak IIF, III) require closer monitoring or surgical evaluation
benign kidney cyst.when_to_seek_help: Consult a urologist or nephrologist for cysts causing flank pain, hematuria (blood in urine), hypertension, or if imaging features are complex or suspicious for malignancy.

immediate_advice: Avoid straining activities that increase scrotal pressure during symptomatic periods
Use supportive underwear (scrotal support) for comfort if the hydrocele is large
Seek urgent evaluation to exclude a more serious underlying condition (testicular torsion or tumor)
quick_solutions: Most congenital hydroceles in infants resolve spontaneously by 12–18 months of age without treatment
In adults, a hydrocele causing discomfort or significant enlargement is treated surgically (hydrocelectomy) — highly effective
Aspiration (draining the fluid with a needle) is a temporary measure that often recurs
Ultrasound is essential to confirm the diagnosis and exclude underlying testicular pathology including tumor
hydrocele of the testicle.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent evaluation for sudden painful scrotal swelling (could indicate testicular torsion — a surgical emergency), fever with swelling (epididymo-orchitis), or rapid increase in size.

immediate_advice: Maintain gentle activity — prolonged bed rest is harmful and delays recovery
Apply heat to the affected area for 15–20 minutes to relieve muscle spasm and stiffness
Avoid heavy lifting, bending at the waist, and twisting movements during acute flares
quick_solutions: NSAIDs are effective for pain management during flares
Physiotherapy focusing on core stabilization, flexibility, and postural correction is the most important long-term intervention
Ergonomic assessment of workstation and sleeping position can significantly reduce daily symptom burden
Surgical intervention (disc replacement, fusion) is reserved for severe, refractory cases with neurological compromise
degenerative disc disease.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for pain radiating to the arms or legs, numbness or tingling in the limbs, progressive weakness, or any bladder or bowel dysfunction which may indicate nerve root or spinal cord compression.

immediate_advice: Seek medical evaluation promptly — hydronephrosis may indicate urinary tract obstruction requiring urgent intervention
Stay well hydrated unless advised otherwise by your doctor
Report any fever, severe flank pain, decreased urine output, or blood in urine immediately
quick_solutions: Identify and treat the underlying cause: kidney stone, ureteral stricture, prostate enlargement, or pelvic mass
Ureteral stenting or percutaneous nephrostomy may be required to relieve obstruction urgently
Antibiotics are required if there is concurrent infection (pyonephrosis) — this combination is a urological emergency
Regular imaging follow-up is required to monitor renal function and degree of obstruction
hydronephrosis.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for severe flank pain with fever and chills (obstructed infected kidney), sudden complete absence of urine output, or worsening kidney function on blood tests.

immediate_advice: Increase dietary fiber intake gradually to 25–35g per day through whole grains, fruits, vegetables, and legumes
Stay well hydrated — at least 2 liters of fluid daily to support fiber's effect on stool consistency
Maintain regular physical activity which promotes healthy bowel motility
quick_solutions: High-fiber diet and adequate hydration are the cornerstones of preventing diverticulosis from progressing to diverticulitis
Fiber supplements (psyllium husk) are useful if dietary fiber targets cannot be met through food alone
Avoid prolonged straining at stool — use a footstool to adopt a squatting position if helpful
The older advice to avoid nuts, seeds, and popcorn is not evidence-based — these do not increase diverticulitis risk
diverticulosis.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for significant rectal bleeding, new onset lower abdominal pain, change in bowel habits, or if diverticulosis was found incidentally to discuss colonoscopy surveillance intervals.

immediate_advice: Take prescribed analgesia as directed and on schedule — do not wait for pain to become severe before taking the next dose
Report pain that is severe, sudden, worsening, or out of proportion to the expected post-operative course to your surgeon immediately
Elevate the operated area where possible and apply ice wrapped in a cloth to reduce swelling
quick_solutions: A multimodal analgesia approach (paracetamol + NSAID + weak opioid if needed) is more effective than any single agent
Early mobilization as directed by your surgical team reduces pain, prevents complications, and speeds recovery
Wound care as instructed prevents infection which significantly worsens post-operative pain
Physiotherapy and breathing exercises specific to your procedure support optimal recovery
pain after an operation.when_to_seek_help: Contact your surgeon urgently for sudden increase in pain after initial improvement, fever above 38°C, wound redness, swelling or discharge, shortness of breath, or any symptoms suggesting surgical complications.
pain after an operation.specialist_recommendation: Surgeon (who performed the operation) / Pain Management Specialist

immediate_advice: Move immediately out of the sun and into shade or an air-conditioned environment
Apply cool (not cold) water compresses or take a cool bath to reduce skin temperature and burning sensation
Apply fragrance-free aloe vera gel or a cooling after-sun lotion to the affected skin
quick_solutions: NSAIDs (ibuprofen) taken promptly reduce both pain and the inflammatory response of sunburn
Stay well hydrated as sunburn draws fluid to the skin surface, causing systemic dehydration
Apply broad-spectrum SPF 50+ sunscreen every 2 hours when outdoors and avoid sun exposure between 10am and 4pm
Wear UPF-rated protective clothing, wide-brimmed hats, and UV-blocking sunglasses for ongoing prevention
dermatitis due to sun exposure.when_to_seek_help: Seek medical attention for sunburn covering large areas with blistering, facial swelling, fever, severe headache, confusion, nausea, or if the person is a young child or elderly — these suggest systemic sun toxicity or severe second-degree sunburn.

immediate_advice: Use preservative-free lubricating eye drops regularly to reduce the irritation, redness, and foreign body sensation
Wear UV-protective wrap-around sunglasses outdoors at all times — UV exposure drives pterygium growth
Avoid dusty, windy, and smoky environments which irritate the conjunctiva and accelerate progression
quick_solutions: Mild topical vasoconstrictor or anti-inflammatory eye drops may be used short-term for symptom relief
Surgical excision with conjunctival autografting is the definitive treatment and has lower recurrence rates than excision alone
Surgery is recommended when the pterygium encroaches on the visual axis, causes significant astigmatism, or is cosmetically unacceptable
Sun protection is the most important preventive measure to stop progression after surgical removal
pterygium.when_to_seek_help: See an ophthalmologist if the pterygium is growing toward the pupil, causing blurred vision, inducing significant astigmatism, or causing persistent discomfort not relieved by lubricating drops.

immediate_advice: Keep the affected area clean and dry — fungi thrive in warm, moist environments
Wear loose, breathable clothing (cotton) and change socks daily if the feet are affected
Avoid sharing towels, clothing, or personal items to prevent spread to others
quick_solutions: Topical antifungal agents (clotrimazole, miconazole, terbinafine) applied twice daily for 2–4 weeks are effective for most cutaneous fungal infections
Continue treatment for the full prescribed course even if symptoms resolve early — premature cessation leads to recurrence
For extensive involvement or if topical treatment fails, oral antifungals (terbinafine, fluconazole, itraconazole) are required
Treat all areas simultaneously (for example, treat both feet and footwear for tinea pedis) to prevent reinfection
fungal infection of the skin.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor if the infection is not improving after 2 weeks of topical treatment, if it is widespread, if the scalp or nails are involved (require oral treatment), or if you are immunocompromised.

immediate_advice: Apply the RICE protocol: Rest, Ice (wrapped in cloth for 20 minutes every 2 hours), Compression bandage, Elevation above heart level
Do not bear weight on the injured leg if fracture, significant ligament injury, or severe pain is present
Seek emergency evaluation if there is visible deformity, severe swelling, or neurovascular compromise (pallor, absence of pulse)
quick_solutions: X-ray is required to exclude fracture before commencing soft tissue injury management
NSAIDs reduce pain and swelling effectively after confirmed soft tissue injury
Physiotherapy is critical for full recovery of muscle, ligament, and tendon injuries
Graduated return to activity under physiotherapy guidance prevents re-injury
injury to the leg.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for visible deformity, inability to bear weight, absent pulse in the foot, pallor or cyanosis below the injury, open fracture, or rapidly expanding hematoma.

immediate_advice: Avoid strenuous physical exertion if symptoms include breathlessness, palpitations, or chest pain
Take all prescribed medications (diuretics, ACE inhibitors, beta-blockers, anticoagulants) without omission
Seek emergency care for sudden severe breathlessness, palpitations with hemodynamic compromise, or acute chest pain
quick_solutions: Regular echocardiographic surveillance is essential to monitor valve severity and timing surgical intervention
Diuretics manage fluid overload symptoms; beta-blockers and rate-control agents manage atrial fibrillation
Anticoagulation with warfarin or DOAC is required in mitral stenosis with atrial fibrillation
Valve repair or replacement is recommended when echocardiographic thresholds for severe disease are met or symptoms develop
mitral valve disease.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for sudden severe breathlessness at rest, acute pulmonary edema, hemoptysis, new palpitations with fast heart rate, or syncope.

immediate_advice: Do not stop or suddenly reduce anti-Parkinsonian medications — abrupt withdrawal can cause life-threatening Parkinsonism-hyperpyrexia syndrome
Ensure the environment is fall-safe: remove rugs, install grab rails, use non-slip mats in the bathroom
Take levodopa doses at consistent times relative to meals — high-protein meals can significantly reduce levodopa absorption
quick_solutions: Physiotherapy focusing on gait training, balance exercises, and fall prevention is the most important non-pharmacological intervention
Speech and language therapy (Lee Silverman Voice Treatment) significantly improves hypophonia and swallowing
Regular medication review by a Parkinson's specialist nurse or neurologist optimizes motor control and reduces fluctuations
Deep brain stimulation (DBS) is an effective surgical option for eligible patients with motor fluctuations not controlled by medication
parkinson disease.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent care for missed medication doses causing severe rigidity, swallowing difficulty with aspiration risk, acute confusion, falls causing injury, or any sudden change in motor function or cognition.

immediate_advice: Rest the affected joint and elevate it above heart level to reduce swelling and pain
Apply ice wrapped in a cloth to the joint for 20–30 minutes several times daily during acute attacks
Begin NSAIDs (indomethacin, naproxen, or ibuprofen) at full dose as soon as an attack begins — early treatment significantly shortens duration
quick_solutions: Colchicine is highly effective when taken early in an attack — follow the prescribed low-dose regimen
Oral corticosteroids are an effective alternative if NSAIDs and colchicine are contraindicated
Stay well hydrated with water — dehydration concentrates uric acid and precipitates attacks
Urate-lowering therapy (allopurinol, febuxostat) is initiated after the acute attack resolves for patients with recurrent gout — target serum urate below 360 µmol/L
gout.when_to_seek_help: See a doctor to confirm diagnosis and initiate urate-lowering therapy for recurrent attacks (2 or more per year), tophi, uric acid kidney stones, or if NSAIDs are contraindicated due to kidney disease or peptic ulcer.

immediate_advice: Administer age-appropriate paracetamol or ibuprofen for pain and fever — this is the immediate priority
Apply a warm cloth against the ear for comfort
Keep the child upright as much as possible to promote Eustachian tube drainage
quick_solutions: Watchful waiting with analgesia is appropriate for children over 2 years with mild to moderate symptoms for up to 72 hours
Amoxicillin is first-line antibiotic if treatment is required — complete the full 5–10 day course
Decongestants and antihistamines provide no benefit and are not recommended in children
Recurrent episodes (4+ per year) warrant ENT referral for grommets to prevent hearing loss and language delay
otitis media.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent care for fever above 39°C, severe ear pain, ear discharge, swelling behind the ear (mastoiditis), facial weakness, significant hearing loss, or a child who appears very unwell.
otitis media.specialist_recommendation: General Practitioner / Pediatrician / Otolaryngologist (ENT) for recurrent cases

immediate_advice: For suspected opioid overdose: call emergency services immediately, administer naloxone (nasal spray or injection) if available, and place the person in the recovery position if unconscious but breathing
Do not leave the person alone — naloxone wears off in 30–90 minutes and re-sedation can occur before emergency services arrive
Do not attempt to induce vomiting or give stimulants to an unconscious person
quick_solutions: Medication-assisted treatment (MAT) with buprenorphine or methadone is the most evidence-based treatment for opioid use disorder and significantly reduces overdose mortality
Naltrexone (extended-release injection) is an effective alternative for patients who have completed detoxification
Medically supervised withdrawal management is required for physical opioid dependence — home detox is dangerous
Peer support, psychosocial counseling, and a structured recovery program are essential alongside pharmacotherapy
drug abuse (opioids).when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for suspected overdose (unresponsive, slow or absent breathing, blue lips). Seek urgent addiction medicine assessment for anyone wishing to stop opioids safely.

immediate_advice: Seek emergency medical evaluation immediately — AKI can progress rapidly to life-threatening hyperkalemia and fluid overload
Stop all nephrotoxic medications immediately: NSAIDs, aminoglycoside antibiotics, ACE inhibitors, and contrast agents if recently used
Monitor urine output closely — less than 0.5 ml/kg/hour is a warning sign requiring urgent escalation
quick_solutions: IV fluid resuscitation is the cornerstone of treatment for pre-renal AKI caused by dehydration or sepsis
Identify and urgently treat the underlying cause: obstruction (catheter or stent), infection, nephrotoxins, or shock
Strict fluid balance monitoring, daily electrolyte checks, and renal function tests guide management
Dialysis (hemodialysis or CRRT) is initiated for AKI with severe hyperkalemia, acidosis, fluid overload, or uremia
acute kidney injury.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for markedly reduced or absent urine output, severe flank pain, confusion, muscle weakness, or any known nephrotoxic exposure with rising creatinine.

immediate_advice: Rest — avoid strenuous physical activity, heavy lifting, and sexual intercourse until bleeding has settled and you have been assessed
Seek same-day medical or obstetric evaluation for any vaginal bleeding during pregnancy
Do not take NSAIDs, aspirin, or any medication not prescribed by your obstetrician
quick_solutions: Urgent pelvic ultrasound is required to confirm fetal viability, heartbeat, and exclude ectopic pregnancy
Progesterone supplementation (vaginal or oral) may be prescribed in certain circumstances to support the pregnancy
Rhesus negative mothers require anti-D immunoglobulin within 72 hours of any vaginal bleeding
Psychological support is important — the anxiety of threatened miscarriage is significant and should be acknowledged and addressed
threatened pregnancy.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for heavy bleeding (soaking more than one pad per hour), severe cramping, passage of tissue, dizziness or faintness, or severe one-sided abdominal pain which may indicate ectopic pregnancy.
threatened pregnancy.specialist_recommendation: Obstetrician / Midwife / Early Pregnancy Assessment Unit

immediate_advice: Brush teeth twice daily with fluoride toothpaste using a soft-bristled toothbrush at a 45-degree angle to the gum line
Floss or use interdental brushes once daily to remove plaque from between teeth where toothbrush bristles cannot reach
Rinse with an antiseptic mouthwash (chlorhexidine) for acute gum inflammation — limit use to 2 weeks to prevent staining
quick_solutions: Professional dental cleaning (scaling and root planing) removes calculus and bacterial biofilm below the gum line that brushing cannot address
Attend regular dental check-ups every 6 months for early detection and management of gum disease progression
Stop smoking immediately — tobacco use is the single greatest modifiable risk factor for periodontal disease
Manage diabetes well — poor glycemic control and gum disease have a bidirectional relationship
gum disease.when_to_seek_help: See a dentist promptly for bleeding gums that persist beyond 2 weeks of improved brushing, gum recession, loose teeth, persistent bad breath, pain or swelling, or any pus around the gum line.

immediate_advice: Call emergency services immediately for any significant gastrointestinal bleeding — do not drive yourself
Do not eat or drink anything while awaiting emergency care
Lie flat if feeling dizzy or faint to maintain cerebral perfusion
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — IV access, fluid resuscitation, blood typing and cross-matching are initiated immediately in hospital
Urgent endoscopy (upper or lower) is required within 24 hours for most significant GI bleeds for diagnosis and therapeutic hemostasis
Proton pump inhibitor infusion is initiated immediately for suspected upper GI bleeding from peptic ulcer
Stop all anticoagulants, antiplatelet agents, and NSAIDs immediately and discuss reversal with the treating team
gastrointestinal hemorrhage.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for vomiting blood, passing large amounts of bright red blood rectally, black tarry stools, dizziness, faintness, or collapse — all indicate significant hemorrhage.
gastrointestinal hemorrhage.specialist_recommendation: Gastroenterologist / General Surgeon / Interventional Radiologist (Emergency evaluation first)

immediate_advice: Use an antifungal shampoo (ketoconazole 2%, selenium sulfide, or zinc pyrithione) on the scalp at least twice weekly — leave on for 5 minutes before rinsing
Avoid scratching scales as this worsens inflammation and can introduce secondary bacterial infection
Reduce triggers: stress, cold dry weather, and infrequent shampooing all worsen seborrheic dermatitis
quick_solutions: Topical antifungal creams (ketoconazole, ciclopirox) applied to affected non-scalp areas are highly effective
Short courses of mild topical corticosteroids (hydrocortisone 1%) reduce acute inflammation on the face — avoid long-term use on facial skin
Emollient moisturizers help manage dryness and scale accumulation between treatment applications
Seborrheic dermatitis is a chronic relapsing condition — maintenance treatment (weekly antifungal shampoo) prevents recurrence
seborrheic dermatitis.when_to_seek_help: Consult a dermatologist if the rash is extensive, spreading to the body, not responding to antifungal shampoos after 4 weeks, or if there is significant skin thickening, crusting, or secondary infection.

immediate_advice: Call emergency services immediately for methamphetamine-induced psychosis, chest pain, severe agitation, or seizures
Move the person to a calm, quiet, non-stimulating environment to reduce agitation and paranoia
Do not restrain forcefully unless the person is at immediate risk of harming themselves or others — this can precipitate cardiac events
quick_solutions: There is currently no approved pharmacotherapy for methamphetamine use disorder — behavioral interventions are the primary treatment
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Contingency Management are the most evidence-based psychological treatments
Medically supervised management of acute intoxication may require benzodiazepines for agitation and antihypertensives for cardiovascular complications
Residential rehabilitation programs provide structured intensive treatment for severe dependence
drug abuse (methamphetamine).when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for methamphetamine-induced psychosis, chest pain, severe hypertension, hyperthermia, seizures, or cardiovascular collapse. Seek urgent addiction medicine referral for anyone wanting to stop use.
drug abuse (methamphetamine).specialist_recommendation: Addiction Psychiatrist / Emergency Physician / Addiction Medicine Specialist

immediate_advice: Apply heat to the affected neck muscles for 15–20 minutes to reduce spasm
Gentle passive stretching of the neck toward the opposite side of the tilt can provide relief — do not force movement
Use a supportive pillow that maintains neutral neck alignment during sleep
quick_solutions: NSAIDs and muscle relaxants (diazepam, cyclobenzaprine) are effective for acute-onset torticollis
Physiotherapy with targeted stretching, manual therapy, and postural exercises is the cornerstone of management
Botulinum toxin injections into the affected sternocleidomastoid and trapezius muscles are the most effective treatment for spasmodic (cervical dystonia) torticollis
In infants with congenital muscular torticollis, early physiotherapy stretching prevents permanent postural deformity
torticollis.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent care for torticollis following neck trauma (rule out fracture/subluxation before any manipulation), torticollis in a child with fever (Grisel syndrome), or new onset torticollis in an adult with headache and fever (meningitis).

immediate_advice: Take a simple analgesic (paracetamol or ibuprofen) at the onset of headache — early treatment is more effective than waiting
Apply a warm or cool compress to the forehead and back of the neck
Rest in a quiet, dimly lit room and practice slow, deep breathing to reduce muscle tension
quick_solutions: Identify and manage triggers: poor posture, prolonged screen use, dehydration, irregular meals, sleep deprivation, and stress
Regular neck and shoulder stretching exercises reduce the frequency of tension headaches significantly
Limit analgesic use to fewer than 10 days per month — overuse leads to medication overuse headache which is more difficult to treat
Amitriptyline (low dose, nightly) is effective preventive therapy for frequent tension headaches
tension headache.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for the worst headache of your life (thunderclap headache), headache with fever and neck stiffness, headache after head injury, headache with neurological symptoms, or any sudden change in headache pattern.
tension headache.specialist_recommendation: General Practitioner / Neurologist (for chronic or refractory cases)

immediate_advice: Place the intoxicated person in the recovery position (on their side) to prevent choking on vomit if they are semi-conscious
Call emergency services immediately for unconsciousness, very slow or irregular breathing, pale or blue-tinged skin, hypothermia, or seizure
Do not give coffee or food — these do not accelerate alcohol metabolism and may cause vomiting with risk of aspiration
quick_solutions: There is no antidote for alcohol — supportive care (maintaining airway, temperature, IV fluids if available) is the treatment
Monitor breathing and consciousness level closely until the person is fully alert
Thiamine (B1) supplementation should be given before or alongside any glucose in malnourished or heavy drinkers to prevent Wernicke's encephalopathy
Address the context of heavy episodic drinking with a brief intervention or addiction referral when the person is sober
alcohol intoxication.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for loss of consciousness, very slow or absent breathing, seizure, hypothermia, or suspected co-ingestion of other substances — severe alcohol intoxication is a medical emergency.
alcohol intoxication.specialist_recommendation: Emergency Physician / Addiction Medicine Specialist (for follow-up)

immediate_advice: For anaphylaxis: administer epinephrine (EpiPen) auto-injector immediately into the outer thigh and call emergency services
Remove the allergen from the environment and stop the person from consuming any further suspect food
For mild reactions (hives, itching only), administer an oral antihistamine and monitor closely for any progression
quick_solutions: Strict avoidance of the confirmed allergen is the cornerstone of management — read all food labels carefully
Always carry two epinephrine auto-injectors and ensure family, school staff, and colleagues know how to use them
Formal allergy testing (skin prick test and specific IgE) by an allergist confirms the diagnosis and guides avoidance
Oral immunotherapy (OIT) is an emerging treatment that increases the threshold for allergic reaction in selected patients
food allergy.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for any throat tightening, difficulty breathing, lip or tongue swelling, sudden drop in blood pressure, or loss of consciousness following food ingestion. See an allergist for formal testing after any allergic food reaction.

immediate_advice: Rest in a quiet, darkened room during acute severe vertigo — avoid sudden head movements which worsen symptoms
Take prescribed vestibular suppressants (prochlorperazine, meclizine, or dimenhydrinate) for acute nausea and vertigo
Do not drive, operate machinery, or work at heights until vertigo has fully resolved
quick_solutions: Labyrinthitis is usually viral and self-limiting — most patients improve significantly within 1–3 weeks
Vestibular rehabilitation exercises (Cawthorne-Cooksey exercises) begun once acute phase settles significantly accelerate recovery
Oral corticosteroids may be prescribed for viral labyrinthitis to reduce inner ear inflammation
Antiviral medications are used if herpes zoster oticus (Ramsay Hunt syndrome) is the suspected cause
labyrinthitis.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent neurological evaluation for vertigo associated with severe headache, double vision, difficulty swallowing, limb weakness, or inability to walk — these suggest a central cause (stroke or cerebellar lesion) rather than peripheral labyrinthitis.

immediate_advice: Practice slow diaphragmatic breathing: inhale for 4 counts, hold for 2, exhale slowly for 6 counts — repeat for 5 minutes
Ground yourself using the 5-4-3-2-1 technique: name 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, 3 you can hear, 2 you can smell, 1 you can taste
Move to a calm environment, reduce sensory stimulation, and avoid caffeine and stimulants
quick_solutions: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) is the most evidence-based treatment for all anxiety disorders and produces durable long-term outcomes
Regular aerobic exercise (30 minutes, 5 times per week) reduces anxiety severity comparably to medication in clinical trials
SSRIs (sertraline, escitalopram) and SNRIs are effective first-line pharmacological treatments when therapy alone is insufficient
Mindfulness-based stress reduction (MBSR) programs have robust evidence for reducing anxiety and preventing relapse
anxiety.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor or mental health professional if anxiety is persistent (most days for more than 2 weeks), is significantly impairing work, relationships, or daily function, or if associated with panic attacks, phobias, or intrusive thoughts.

immediate_advice: Remove access to triggers where possible: limit gambling sites, restrict access to alcohol, remove sharp objects if self-harm is a risk
Implement a delay strategy — commit to waiting 15–30 minutes before acting on an impulse, as urges typically peak and subside
Seek urgent psychiatric assessment if impulse control failures are leading to harm to self or others
quick_solutions: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) with specific impulse control modules is the primary treatment approach
Habit reversal training is effective for trichotillomania, skin picking, and similar repetitive behavior disorders
Mood stabilizers or SSRIs may be prescribed for impulse control disorders with co-occurring mood or OCD-spectrum features
Motivational interviewing helps build the intrinsic motivation required for sustained behavioral change
impulse control disorder.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent psychiatric care if impulse control failures have resulted in significant harm to self or others, legal consequences, financial devastation, or if there is co-occurring suicidality or psychosis.

immediate_advice: Do not squeeze or attempt to drain a nasal abscess yourself — the facial venous system drains directly into the cavernous sinus and manipulation can spread infection intracranially
Apply warm compresses to reduce discomfort while awaiting medical review
Seek prompt medical evaluation — nasal and facial abscesses require urgent professional management
quick_solutions: Seek urgent medical evaluation — incision and drainage under appropriate anesthesia is the definitive treatment
Systemic antibiotics (covering Staphylococcus aureus, including MRSA if community-acquired) are required alongside drainage
Monitor closely for signs of spreading infection: escalating fever, facial swelling, visual changes, headache, or reduced consciousness
Nasal septal abscess is a surgical emergency requiring same-day drainage to prevent septal necrosis and saddle nose deformity
abscess of nose.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for fever with facial swelling, visual changes, headache, peri-orbital swelling, or any altered consciousness in the context of a nasal abscess — these indicate potentially life-threatening spread.

immediate_advice: Begin oral prednisolone (corticosteroids) as soon as possible — treatment within 72 hours of onset significantly improves recovery
Protect the eye that cannot close fully: use lubricating eye drops during the day and eye ointment with a taped eye patch at night to prevent corneal damage
Do not drive if eye closure is incomplete as this significantly impairs vision
quick_solutions: Oral prednisolone (50mg daily for 10 days) is the evidence-based treatment and improves complete recovery rates
Antiviral medications (acyclovir or valacyclovir) are sometimes added alongside steroids though evidence is less definitive
Facial physiotherapy exercises accelerate neuromuscular re-education and improve symmetry during recovery
Most patients (approximately 85%) recover fully within 3–6 months — reassurance is an important part of management
bell palsy.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent medical evaluation for any new onset unilateral facial weakness to exclude stroke. Seek ophthalmological review if the eye cannot close or if redness, pain, or blurred vision develops — corneal ulceration can develop rapidly.
bell palsy.specialist_recommendation: Neurologist / General Practitioner / Ophthalmologist (for eye protection)

immediate_advice: Avoid rubbing the eyes as this releases more histamine and worsens itching and swelling
Apply cool compresses to closed eyelids for 10–15 minutes for immediate symptom relief
Identify and reduce exposure to the triggering allergen: pollen, pet dander, dust mites, or mold
quick_solutions: Topical antihistamine/mast cell stabilizer eye drops (olopatadine, ketotifen) provide rapid and effective relief and are the first-line treatment
Oral antihistamines (cetirizine, loratadine) help if both nasal and ocular symptoms are present (allergic rhinoconjunctivitis)
Preservative-free lubricating eye drops used frequently help dilute and wash away allergens from the ocular surface
Allergen immunotherapy reduces long-term sensitivity and decreases the severity of seasonal allergic conjunctivitis
conjunctivitis due to allergy.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor if symptoms are severe and not responding to topical antihistamines, if vision is affected, if there is significant pain (not just itch), or if symptoms are present year-round and affecting quality of life.

immediate_advice: Stop the suspected causative medication immediately if it is safe to do so and seek medical advice before restarting
For anaphylaxis (throat swelling, difficulty breathing, collapse): administer epinephrine and call emergency services immediately
Document the suspected drug, the reaction description, and the date for future medical records
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately for anaphylaxis, Stevens-Johnson syndrome (widespread skin blistering with mucosal involvement), or DRESS syndrome (rash with fever, lymphadenopathy, and organ involvement)
Mild urticarial drug rashes are managed with antihistamines and monitoring for progression
All suspected drug reactions should be documented and reported to the prescribing physician for formal allergy recording
Allergy testing or drug provocation challenge under specialist supervision may be arranged to confirm or refute the diagnosis
drug reaction.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for difficulty breathing, throat swelling, widespread blistering of skin or mucous membranes, fever with rash and joint pain, or any systemic drug reaction affecting multiple organs.
drug reaction.specialist_recommendation: Allergist / Immunologist / Dermatologist / Emergency Physician (for severe reactions)

immediate_advice: Schedule an optometrist appointment for refraction testing and updated prescription spectacles or contact lenses
Avoid driving if uncorrected vision is insufficient for safe road use
In children, reduce prolonged near work time and increase outdoor activity to at least 2 hours daily — outdoor light exposure slows myopia progression
quick_solutions: Corrective lenses (spectacles or contact lenses) provide immediate and effective vision correction
Myopia control options for children include orthokeratology, atropine eye drops, and multifocal contact lenses — discuss with an optometrist or ophthalmologist
LASIK, PRK, or SMILE refractive surgery permanently corrects myopia in suitable adults (typically stable prescription for 2+ years)
Regular eye examinations every 12 months ensure the prescription is accurate and monitor for myopia-related complications (retinal detachment, glaucoma)
myopia.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent ophthalmological evaluation for sudden increase in floaters, flashing lights, or a shadow or curtain in the visual field — high myopes are at significantly increased risk of retinal detachment.

immediate_advice: Apply ice for acute flares (first 24–48 hours) to reduce swelling, then switch to heat for muscle relaxation
Rest the affected joint during severe flares but maintain gentle movement as complete immobility worsens stiffness and muscle wasting
Use a walking aid (stick, crutches) if lower limb joint pain is significantly affecting gait and balance
quick_solutions: Regular low-impact exercise (swimming, cycling, walking) is the single most effective long-term intervention — it reduces pain and improves function
Weight reduction in overweight patients reduces joint loading and significantly improves pain and mobility
Physiotherapy with targeted joint strengthening and proprioception training reduces pain and delays disease progression
Intra-articular corticosteroid injections provide effective short-term relief for moderate-to-severe flares
osteoarthritis.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for rapidly worsening pain, sudden joint swelling with redness and warmth (may indicate gout or septic arthritis), significant restriction of daily activities, or when conservative management is failing and joint replacement becomes a consideration.

immediate_advice: Seek urgent ophthalmological evaluation for any sudden change in central vision, new blind spots, or distortion of straight lines
Use the Amsler grid test daily (if provided by your ophthalmologist) to monitor for new distortion and report changes promptly
Protect eyes with UV-blocking sunglasses as UV exposure contributes to disease progression
quick_solutions: Anti-VEGF injections (ranibizumab, aflibercept, bevacizumab) given intravitreally are the standard treatment for wet AMD and preserve or improve vision in the majority of treated patients
High-dose AREDS2 antioxidant supplements (zinc, vitamins C and E, lutein, zeaxanthin) reduce progression risk in intermediate dry AMD
Quit smoking immediately — smoking is the most significant modifiable risk factor for AMD and doubles disease risk
Low vision aids (magnifiers, reading aids, screen enlargement software) maintain independence and quality of life for those with significant vision loss
macular degeneration.when_to_seek_help: Seek same-day urgent ophthalmological care for any sudden deterioration of central vision, new distortion of straight lines, or appearance of a dark spot in central vision — wet AMD can progress rapidly and treatment delay results in permanent vision loss.

immediate_advice: Seek medical evaluation promptly — pneumonia requires clinical assessment and may need antibiotics, hospitalization, or oxygen support
Rest, stay well hydrated, and use paracetamol or ibuprofen for fever and pleuritic chest pain relief
Sit upright — this position maximizes diaphragmatic excursion and ventilation efficiency
quick_solutions: Antibiotics are required for bacterial pneumonia — amoxicillin is first-line for community-acquired pneumonia in most patients
Complete the full antibiotic course even if symptoms improve within 2–3 days
Annual influenza vaccine and pneumococcal vaccine significantly reduce pneumonia risk in high-risk groups
Follow-up chest X-ray at 6–8 weeks is recommended, particularly in smokers and older adults, to confirm resolution and exclude underlying malignancy
pneumonia.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for severe breathlessness, oxygen saturation below 94%, confusion, very high fever above 40°C, rapid heart rate above 120/min, blue lips, or inability to maintain hydration — these indicate severe or complicated pneumonia.
pneumonia.specialist_recommendation: Pulmonologist / Infectious Disease Specialist / General Physician

immediate_advice: Avoid known triggers: caffeine, alcohol, nicotine, energy drinks, and significant sleep deprivation
Manage stress and anxiety as sympathetic nervous system activation reliably increases PVC burden
Seek urgent evaluation if PVCs are frequent, causing significant palpitations, or associated with dizziness or presyncope
quick_solutions: Most PVCs in structurally normal hearts are benign and require only reassurance and trigger avoidance
A 24–48 hour Holter monitor quantifies PVC burden and identifies any associated arrhythmias
Echocardiogram is required to exclude structural heart disease — PVCs in the context of cardiomyopathy require specialist management
Beta-blockers or calcium channel blockers are used for symptomatic PVCs not responding to lifestyle modification
premature ventricular contractions (pvcs.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent cardiology evaluation for PVCs occurring in runs (non-sustained ventricular tachycardia), PVCs with syncope or near-syncope, PVC burden greater than 10–20% on Holter monitoring, or any PVCs in a patient with known heart disease.

immediate_advice: Eat soft foods (yogurt, soup, scrambled eggs) and avoid hard, chewy foods that require wide jaw opening
Apply warm compresses to the jaw joint and surrounding muscles for 15–20 minutes several times daily
Avoid clenching or grinding teeth — place your tongue lightly between teeth as a reminder to keep jaw muscles relaxed
quick_solutions: NSAIDs provide effective short-term pain and inflammation relief
A custom-fitted occlusal splint (nightguard) worn during sleep reduces clenching, grinding, and joint loading
Physiotherapy targeting jaw muscle exercises, postural correction, and manual therapy is highly effective
Stress management and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy address the anxiety and bruxism that often underlie TMJ disorder
temporomandibular joint disorder.when_to_seek_help: Consult a dentist or oral and maxillofacial surgeon for jaw locking (inability to open or close the mouth), significant clicking or popping with pain, worsening restriction of mouth opening, or pain not improving with conservative measures.
temporomandibular joint disorder.specialist_recommendation: Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeon / Dentist / Physiotherapist

immediate_advice: Take prescribed or OTC analgesia (paracetamol and NSAIDs) regularly and consistently — adequate pain control is essential to maintain deep breathing
Hold a pillow firmly against the ribs when coughing or taking deep breaths — this splinting reduces pain significantly
Seek emergency care for multiple rib fractures, severe breathing difficulty, or any suspected underlying organ injury
quick_solutions: Adequate analgesia is the cornerstone of rib fracture management — pain control enables deep breathing and prevents pneumonia
Perform deep breathing exercises (incentive spirometry if available) hourly when awake to prevent atelectasis and pneumonia
Avoid tight bandaging or strapping of the chest — this restricts breathing and increases pneumonia risk
Hospital admission and epidural analgesia are recommended for patients with 3 or more rib fractures, elderly patients, or those with pre-existing lung disease
fracture of the rib.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for severe breathing difficulty, low oxygen saturation, three or more fractured ribs, suspected pneumothorax or hemothorax, or a first rib fracture (indicates high-energy trauma with risk of major vessel injury).

immediate_advice: Stop the causative agent immediately — identify and remove the offending drug, supplement, herbal product, or toxin
Seek emergency care immediately for jaundice (yellow skin/eyes), confusion, or severe abdominal pain
Avoid all alcohol, NSAIDs, paracetamol, and any additional hepatotoxic substances
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately for suspected severe drug-induced liver injury — N-acetylcysteine is used for paracetamol toxicity and must be started early
Liver function tests, INR, and bilirubin must be monitored closely — rising INR indicates severe liver dysfunction
Stop all non-essential medications and report all supplements, herbal remedies, and over-the-counter medications to your doctor
Liver transplant assessment should be initiated early if acute liver failure is developing
hepatitis due to a toxin.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for jaundice, confusion or altered behavior (hepatic encephalopathy), severe abdominal pain, easy bruising, or coagulopathy — these indicate acute liver failure which is rapidly fatal without intervention.

immediate_advice: Most small vaginal cysts are asymptomatic and require no immediate action
Maintain good vulvovaginal hygiene using warm water only — avoid douches and scented products
Seek evaluation if the cyst is growing, becomes painful, or causes difficulty with intercourse or hygiene
quick_solutions: Warm sitz baths 3–4 times daily can help a Bartholin's gland cyst drain spontaneously if small and mildly symptomatic
Bartholin's abscess requires surgical drainage (marsupialisation or Word catheter insertion) — do not attempt self-drainage
Most inclusion cysts or Gartner's duct cysts are benign and managed expectantly with periodic monitoring
Surgical excision is recommended for cysts that are enlarging, symptomatic, recurrent, or if malignancy cannot be excluded
vaginal cyst.when_to_seek_help: Consult a gynecologist for any vaginal cyst that is painful, rapidly enlarging, causing urinary or bowel symptoms, or associated with discharge or bleeding. Any cyst in a postmenopausal woman requires prompt evaluation.

immediate_advice: Call emergency services or go to the emergency department immediately — ectopic pregnancy is a life-threatening emergency
Do not eat or drink anything in case emergency surgery is required
Lie flat if feeling dizzy or faint to maintain blood pressure
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — this is a surgical emergency; ruptured ectopic pregnancy causes life-threatening internal hemorrhage
Diagnosis is confirmed with serum beta-hCG and transvaginal ultrasound
Medical management with methotrexate is possible for small unruptured ectopics — must be done under close specialist supervision
Surgical management (laparoscopic salpingectomy or salpingostomy) is required for ruptured or large ectopic pregnancies
ectopic pregnancy.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for severe one-sided abdominal pain, vaginal bleeding with a positive pregnancy test, pain radiating to the shoulder tip (diaphragmatic irritation from blood), dizziness, faintness, or collapse.
ectopic pregnancy.specialist_recommendation: Obstetrician / Gynecologist / Emergency Surgeon (Emergency services first)

immediate_advice: Do not attempt abrupt cessation of heavy alcohol use without medical supervision — alcohol withdrawal can cause life-threatening seizures and delirium tremens
If the person is intoxicated to the point of reduced consciousness, call emergency services immediately
Remove access to alcohol from the immediate environment if safe to do so
quick_solutions: Medically supervised detoxification using chlordiazepoxide or diazepam is the safe management for alcohol withdrawal
Thiamine supplementation is essential in all alcohol-dependent patients to prevent irreversible Wernicke-Korsakoff syndrome
Naltrexone or acamprosate are evidence-based medications that reduce craving and relapse rates after detoxification
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, motivational interviewing, and peer support groups (Alcoholics Anonymous) are essential long-term treatment components
alcohol abuse.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for seizures, severe confusion or hallucinations during withdrawal (delirium tremens), vomiting blood, jaundice, or severe abdominal pain. Seek urgent addiction medicine assessment for safe medically supervised detoxification.
alcohol abuse.specialist_recommendation: Addiction Medicine Specialist / Addiction Psychiatrist / Hepatologist (for liver disease)

immediate_advice: Wear a neutral wrist splint at night — this is the single most effective first-line intervention and significantly reduces nocturnal symptoms
Avoid activities that involve prolonged wrist flexion or extension: typing with bent wrists, gripping vibrating tools, or resting wrists on hard surfaces
Apply ice to the wrist for 10–15 minutes to reduce inflammation during acute flares
quick_solutions: Ergonomic adjustments to workstation setup: keyboard and mouse at elbow height, wrist in neutral position
Corticosteroid injection into the carpal tunnel provides effective medium-term symptom relief and delays or avoids surgery
Surgical carpal tunnel release (open or endoscopic) is highly effective and curative for moderate-to-severe cases — a short day-procedure
Nerve conduction studies confirm the diagnosis and severity, guiding the decision between conservative and surgical management
carpal tunnel syndrome.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for persistent numbness, tingling, or weakness in the thumb, index, and middle fingers — particularly if nocturnal symptoms are disrupting sleep or if thumb muscles are visibly wasting (thenar atrophy requires urgent referral).

immediate_advice: Drink large amounts of water (aim for 2–3 liters daily) to help flush the stone through the urinary tract
Take NSAIDs (ibuprofen, diclofenac) at full dose for pain — they are more effective than opioids for ureteric colic
Seek emergency care for severe uncontrolled pain, fever with pain (obstructed infected kidney is a urological emergency), or if only one functioning kidney is present
quick_solutions: Strain urine through a fine sieve to capture the stone for laboratory analysis to guide prevention
Alpha-blockers (tamsulosin) facilitate spontaneous passage of distal ureteric stones
Stones larger than 10mm rarely pass spontaneously — ureteroscopy, shock wave lithotripsy, or percutaneous nephrolithotomy may be required
Dietary modifications specific to stone type (reduce oxalate, salt, and animal protein; maintain adequate calcium) prevent recurrence
kidney stone.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for fever and chills with flank pain (infected obstructed kidney), uncontrolled pain despite analgesics, inability to keep fluids down, or known single functioning kidney with suspected obstruction.

immediate_advice: Maintain a predictable routine and structured environment — unexpected changes cause significant distress and should be minimized
Use clear, literal communication and avoid idioms, sarcasm, or ambiguous language
Provide a quiet sensory refuge space the person can access when overwhelmed by sensory stimulation
quick_solutions: Early intensive behavioral intervention (Applied Behavior Analysis) significantly improves communication, adaptive skills, and independence in young children
Speech and language therapy addresses communication deficits and augmentative communication strategies
Occupational therapy for sensory processing and daily living skills improves independence and quality of life
Address co-occurring conditions proactively: anxiety, ADHD, epilepsy, and sleep disorders are all common in autistic individuals and are treatable
autism.when_to_seek_help: Consult a developmental pediatrician or child psychiatrist for formal assessment if developmental regression occurs, communication is significantly delayed, repetitive behaviors are causing self-harm, or if there are safety concerns related to behavior.
autism.specialist_recommendation: Developmental Pediatrician / Child Psychiatrist / Speech Therapist / Occupational Therapist

immediate_advice: Use prescribed intraocular pressure-lowering eye drops exactly as directed — never skip doses as consistent pressure control prevents progression
Attend all follow-up appointments for visual field testing and optic nerve assessment
Inform all healthcare providers of your glaucoma diagnosis as some medications (topical and systemic steroids, anticholinergics) can raise intraocular pressure
quick_solutions: Prostaglandin analogue eye drops (latanoprost, bimatoprost) are first-line and most effective for IOP reduction
Laser trabeculoplasty (SLT) can lower IOP and reduce the medication burden — suitable for many patients
Surgical trabeculectomy or minimally invasive glaucoma surgery (MIGS) is recommended when medical or laser treatment is insufficient
Regular visual field testing every 6–12 months monitors disease progression and guides treatment intensification
chronic glaucoma.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent ophthalmological care for any sudden change in vision, sudden eye pain, halos around lights, or if you notice your visual field is narrowing. Report any difficulty with eye drops to your ophthalmologist as adherence is critical.

immediate_advice: Seek emergency ophthalmological care immediately — retinal detachment is a surgical emergency and treatment delay causes permanent vision loss
Do not eat or drink anything in case emergency surgery is required
Lie flat if advised by your ophthalmologist — posturing may slow the progression depending on detachment location
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — pneumatic retinopexy, scleral buckling, or vitrectomy are the surgical options depending on extent and location
Outcomes are significantly better when the macula is still attached — any delay from symptom onset to surgery worsens the visual prognosis
Prophylactic laser retinopexy is performed for retinal tears or lattice degeneration discovered before detachment occurs
High myopes and those with a family history of retinal detachment require regular dilated fundus examination
retinal detachment.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for sudden shower of floaters, flashing lights, or a shadow or curtain spreading across the visual field — these are classic warning signs of retinal tear or detachment requiring same-day surgical evaluation.

immediate_advice: Do not ignore difficulty urinating or a sensation of incomplete bladder emptying — urinary retention causing overflow incontinence requires urgent catheterization if severe
Timed voiding (attempting to urinate every 2–3 hours whether or not the urge is felt) reduces leakage from a chronically overdistended bladder
Avoid medications that can worsen urinary retention: antihistamines, antidepressants, and decongestants
quick_solutions: Treat the underlying cause: bladder outlet obstruction (BPH, urethral stricture) or underactive detrusor (diabetic neuropathy, spinal cord disease)
Clean intermittent self-catheterization (CISC) is taught to patients with chronic urinary retention causing overflow incontinence
Alpha-blockers (tamsulosin) relieve outlet obstruction in BPH; 5-alpha reductase inhibitors (finasteride) reduce prostate volume
Surgical intervention (TURP for BPH, urethral dilatation for stricture) is often required for structural causes of outlet obstruction
overflow incontinence.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent care for complete inability to urinate (acute urinary retention), abdominal pain from a distended bladder, or fever with urinary symptoms suggesting infection with retention.

immediate_advice: Rest from the provocative activity and apply ice wrapped in a cloth to the lateral elbow for 20 minutes, 3–4 times daily
Use a forearm counterforce brace (tennis elbow strap) worn just below the elbow during activities to reduce tendon loading
Avoid gripping, twisting, and lifting activities with the palm facing down during the acute phase
quick_solutions: Physiotherapy with eccentric and isometric wrist extensor strengthening exercises is the most effective long-term treatment
Topical NSAIDs applied directly to the lateral elbow provide localized relief with minimal systemic effects
Corticosteroid injection provides rapid short-term pain relief but does not improve long-term outcomes — use is limited
Most cases resolve within 6–12 months with conservative management — patience and consistency with physiotherapy are essential
lateral epicondylitis (tennis elbow).when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor if pain is severe and affecting sleep or basic daily activities, if there is elbow joint swelling or locking, if symptoms have not improved after 6 weeks of conservative management, or if the diagnosis is uncertain.
lateral epicondylitis (tennis elbow).specialist_recommendation: Orthopedic Surgeon / Physiotherapist / Sports Medicine Doctor

immediate_advice: For nosebleed (epistaxis): sit upright, lean slightly forward, pinch the soft lower part of the nose firmly for 10–15 continuous minutes — do not tilt the head back
For nasal obstruction: use saline irrigation to clear secretions and a short course of nasal decongestant spray (maximum 3 days)
Avoid inserting objects into the nose and do not blow the nose forcefully during the acute phase of any nasal condition
quick_solutions: Intranasal corticosteroid spray (fluticasone, mometasone) is highly effective for allergic and non-allergic rhinitis causing nasal obstruction
Saline nasal irrigation performed daily reduces nasal symptoms significantly across a range of nasal disorders
Cauterization of bleeding vessels (silver nitrate or electrocautery) is performed by an ENT specialist for recurrent nosebleeds
Surgical intervention (septoplasty, turbinate reduction, polypectomy) is available for structural causes of nasal obstruction
nose disorder.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for uncontrolled nosebleeds not stopping after 20–30 minutes of direct pressure, nosebleed after head trauma, or very heavy bleeding. Consult an ENT for recurrent bleeds, one-sided nasal obstruction with blood-stained discharge, or loss of sense of smell.

immediate_advice: Do not attempt unsupervised withdrawal from alcohol, benzodiazepines, or opioids — withdrawal from these substances can cause life-threatening seizures or cardiovascular collapse
Seek urgent medical assessment for safe detoxification planning before stopping these substances
Ensure the person is in a safe, supervised environment with access to immediate medical care if symptoms worsen
quick_solutions: Medically supervised detoxification with appropriate substitution therapy (chlordiazepoxide for alcohol, methadone or buprenorphine for opioids, long-acting benzodiazepines for benzodiazepine withdrawal) is the standard of care
Thiamine supplementation is essential during alcohol detoxification to prevent Wernicke's encephalopathy
Clonidine reduces autonomic symptoms of opioid withdrawal (sweating, agitation, tachycardia)
After detoxification, ongoing medications (naltrexone, acamprosate, buprenorphine maintenance) significantly reduce relapse risk
drug withdrawal.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for seizures, severe confusion or hallucinations, chest pain, or cardiovascular instability during drug withdrawal — these indicate life-threatening complications requiring immediate hospital management.
drug withdrawal.specialist_recommendation: Addiction Medicine Specialist / Addiction Psychiatrist / Emergency Physician

immediate_advice: Brush teeth twice daily with fluoride toothpaste (1450 ppm fluoride for adults) using a soft-bristled toothbrush
For toothache from dental caries: take paracetamol or ibuprofen for pain relief and clove oil applied to the affected area provides temporary topical analgesia
See a dentist as soon as possible — dental caries only worsen without intervention and do not heal spontaneously
quick_solutions: Fluoride varnish applied professionally every 6 months significantly reduces caries progression in high-risk patients
Dietary modification is essential: reduce frequency of sugary and acidic food and drink consumption
Dental restoration (filling) is required for cavitated caries to stop progression and restore tooth structure
Pit and fissure sealants on permanent molar teeth in children are highly effective at preventing caries in the most vulnerable tooth surfaces
dental caries.when_to_seek_help: See a dentist for any toothache, tooth sensitivity to hot, cold, or sweet stimuli, visible dark spots or holes in teeth, or swelling around a tooth. Seek urgent dental care for severe tooth pain, facial swelling, or fever suggesting a dental abscess.

immediate_advice: Seek emergency care immediately for sudden onset reduced or absent urine output, severe fluid retention, confusion, or difficulty breathing
Stop all nephrotoxic medications immediately: NSAIDs, aminoglycosides, ACE inhibitors in acute setting
Follow strict fluid and dietary restrictions as prescribed — do not exceed fluid allowance or eat high-potassium foods
quick_solutions: Dialysis (hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis) is life-sustaining treatment for end-stage kidney failure — adhere strictly to all scheduled sessions
Dietary management with a renal dietitian (protein, potassium, phosphate, fluid restriction) is a critical component of CKD management
Control blood pressure strictly (target below 130/80 mmHg) to slow progression of kidney disease
Kidney transplant evaluation should be pursued for eligible patients as it provides significantly better quality of life than dialysis
kidney failure.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for sudden significant reduction in urine output, severe breathlessness (fluid overload), muscle weakness with palpitations (hyperkalemia), confusion, or missed dialysis sessions.
kidney failure.specialist_recommendation: Nephrologist / Renal Transplant Team / Renal Dietitian

immediate_advice: Gently stretch the affected muscle in the direction opposite the spasm — for calf cramps, flex the foot upward firmly
Apply heat to the affected area for 15–20 minutes to relax the muscle and increase blood flow
Massage the cramping muscle with firm, sustained pressure to encourage relaxation
quick_solutions: Ensure adequate hydration — dehydration is a common precipitant of muscle cramps and spasms
Address electrolyte deficiencies: low magnesium, potassium, and calcium all contribute to muscle cramping
Regular stretching of prone muscle groups (calves, hamstrings) before sleep reduces nocturnal cramp frequency
Muscle relaxants (cyclobenzaprine, baclofen) are prescribed for persistent spasm associated with injury or neurological conditions
muscle spasm.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for very frequent or severe muscle spasms, spasms associated with significant weakness or neurological symptoms, spasms following injury or that do not resolve within a few minutes, or for nocturnal cramps significantly disrupting sleep.

immediate_advice: Seek urgent medical evaluation immediately — peritonsillar abscess requires same-day drainage
Do not take aspirin in children (risk of Reye's syndrome); use paracetamol or ibuprofen for pain while awaiting care
Maintain hydration with cool liquids — swallowing is painful but dehydration must be prevented
quick_solutions: Seek urgent ENT or emergency care immediately — incision and drainage or needle aspiration is the definitive treatment
IV antibiotics (penicillin-based with metronidazole) are initiated after drainage
IV fluid hydration is often required as severe odynophagia prevents adequate oral intake
Tonsillectomy is considered after the second episode to prevent recurrence
peritonsillar abscess.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for drooling (inability to swallow), muffled voice (hot potato voice), severe unilateral throat pain, trismus (inability to open the mouth), uvular deviation, stridor, or difficulty breathing — these indicate impending airway compromise.

immediate_advice: Avoid scratching — use a cool damp cloth pressed firmly against the itchy area to relieve the sensation without breaking the skin
Apply a fragrance-free, hypoallergenic moisturizer immediately after bathing to reduce skin dryness which worsens itch
Wear loose, soft, cotton clothing and avoid wool, synthetic fabrics, and tight waistbands
quick_solutions: Oral antihistamines (cetirizine or loratadine by day; chlorphenamine at night for its sedating effect) reduce itch significantly
Keep nails short and clean to minimize skin damage from unavoidable scratching
Avoid hot showers and baths — use lukewarm water and gentle, fragrance-free cleansers only
Investigate underlying systemic causes: thyroid disease, liver disease, kidney failure, iron deficiency, and certain malignancies all cause generalized pruritus
itching of unknown cause.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for itching lasting more than 6 weeks (chronic pruritus), itching without any visible rash, itching accompanied by weight loss, fatigue, night sweats, or jaundice — these features require investigation for serious systemic disease.

immediate_advice: Seek urgent specialist evaluation — any suspicious head or neck lesion, persistent hoarseness, or neck lump lasting more than 3 weeks requires urgent investigation
Do not delay referral waiting to see if symptoms resolve — early-stage head and neck cancers are highly curable
Maintain nutrition carefully — swallowing difficulties are common and a dietitian referral should be obtained early
quick_solutions: Urgent biopsy and staging imaging (CT/MRI/PET scan) are required to confirm diagnosis and plan treatment
Treatment is multidisciplinary: surgery, radiotherapy, and chemotherapy may all be involved depending on site and stage
Stop smoking and alcohol consumption immediately — both are causative factors and continued use worsens treatment outcomes significantly
Speech therapy and swallowing rehabilitation are integral parts of treatment planning for pharyngeal and laryngeal cancers
head and neck cancer.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent evaluation for a painless neck lump persisting more than 3 weeks, persistent hoarseness beyond 3 weeks, unexplained mouth ulcer lasting more than 3 weeks, difficulty swallowing, unexplained ear pain, or any blood-stained saliva or sputum.
head and neck cancer.specialist_recommendation: Head and Neck Surgeon / Oncologist / Radiation Oncologist

immediate_advice: Do not restrict fluid intake — dehydration concentrates urine which irritates the bladder and worsens urgency
Avoid bladder irritants: caffeine, alcohol, carbonated drinks, artificial sweeteners, and citrus juices
Practice urge suppression: when urgency occurs, stand still or sit down, tighten pelvic floor muscles, and wait for the urge to pass before walking to the toilet
quick_solutions: Bladder retraining (gradually extending the time between voids) is a highly effective behavioral intervention that restructures bladder habits
Pelvic floor muscle training with a specialized physiotherapist reduces urgency episodes significantly
Anticholinergic medications (oxybutynin, solifenacin, tolterodine) or beta-3 agonists (mirabegron) reduce detrusor overactivity
Botulinum toxin bladder injections or sacral neuromodulation are options for refractory overactive bladder
urge incontinence.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor if incontinence is significantly affecting daily life, sleep, or social activities, if there is associated blood in urine, recurrent urinary tract infections, or if bladder training and lifestyle measures have not helped after 3 months.

immediate_advice: Do not rub the eye — this significantly worsens epithelial damage and risk of infection
If a foreign body caused the abrasion, gently irrigate with clean water or saline — do not attempt to remove an embedded object
Remove contact lenses immediately and do not reinsert until fully healed and cleared by a doctor
quick_solutions: Antibiotic eye drops (chloramphenicol or tobramycin) are prescribed to prevent secondary infection
Preservative-free lubricating eye drops reduce friction and support epithelial healing
Most simple corneal abrasions heal completely within 24–72 hours with appropriate treatment
Contact lens-related abrasions require antipseudomonal antibiotic coverage and urgent ophthalmological assessment
corneal abrasion.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent ophthalmological care for severe pain not improving within 24 hours, significantly worsening redness, purulent discharge, or any visible opacity or white spot on the cornea suggesting infection.

immediate_advice: Maintain regular daily routines for sleep, meals, and physical activity even when motivation is low — structure counteracts dysthymia
Identify and limit exposure to negative social media, news, or environments that reinforce low mood
Reach out to a trusted person — social connection is protective and isolation worsens persistent low mood
quick_solutions: Psychotherapy — particularly Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Interpersonal Therapy (IPT) — is highly effective and the preferred first-line treatment
Regular aerobic exercise (30 minutes, 5 days per week) has antidepressant effects comparable to medication in mild-to-moderate depression
SSRIs (sertraline, fluoxetine, escitalopram) are effective pharmacological treatments when psychotherapy alone is insufficient
Combined therapy (psychotherapy plus antidepressant) produces better outcomes than either treatment alone for persistent depressive disorder
dysthymic disorder.when_to_seek_help: Consult a mental health professional for persistent low mood lasting more than 2 years, significant impairment in daily functioning, or if suicidal ideation develops. Seek emergency care immediately for any active suicidal intent or plan.

immediate_advice: Take all prescribed antihypertensive medications consistently — do not skip doses even if feeling well
Restrict sodium intake to less than 2g per day and adopt the DASH diet (rich in fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and low-fat dairy)
Seek emergency care for severe headache, chest pain, shortness of breath, or visual disturbance — these may indicate hypertensive emergency
quick_solutions: Regular blood pressure monitoring at home guides medication adjustments — target below 130/80 mmHg
Regular aerobic exercise (150 minutes per week), weight loss, and smoking cessation all reduce blood pressure and cardiac risk
Echocardiogram is essential to assess left ventricular hypertrophy and diastolic function
