Annual review with your cardiologist including ECG, echocardiogram, and kidney function testing monitors for end-organ damage
hypertensive heart disease.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for blood pressure above 180/120 mmHg with symptoms, chest pain, acute shortness of breath, confusion, vision changes, or neurological symptoms — these indicate hypertensive emergency requiring immediate IV treatment.

immediate_advice: Begin lifestyle modifications immediately — even modest weight loss of 5–10% of body weight significantly restores ovulation and reduces androgen levels
Adopt a low glycemic index diet to reduce insulin resistance which drives the majority of PCOS symptoms
Engage in regular physical activity: aim for at least 150 minutes of moderate aerobic exercise per week
quick_solutions: The combined oral contraceptive pill regulates menstruation and reduces androgen-related symptoms (acne, hirsutism)
Metformin improves insulin sensitivity, regulates cycles, and reduces long-term metabolic risk in PCOS
Clomiphene or letrozole is used for ovulation induction when fertility is desired
Screening for type 2 diabetes, dyslipidemia, and metabolic syndrome should be performed regularly as PCOS significantly increases these risks
polycystic ovarian syndrome (pcos).when_to_seek_help: Consult a gynecologist or endocrinologist for irregular periods, difficulty conceiving, excessive hair growth, persistent acne, or significant weight gain associated with irregular cycles — early treatment prevents long-term metabolic complications.

immediate_advice: Wash the affected area gently with warm water only — avoid soaps, shower gels, and antiseptics which worsen skin irritation
Dry the area gently but thoroughly after washing — moisture promotes fungal and bacterial overgrowth
Avoid sexual intercourse until symptoms have resolved to prevent worsening and partner transmission
quick_solutions: Topical antifungal cream (clotrimazole) applied twice daily for 2 weeks treats the most common cause (Candida balanitis)
Topical low-potency corticosteroid cream reduces inflammation in non-infectious balanitis
Topical antibiotic cream is used if bacterial cause is confirmed or suspected
Recurrent balanitis despite treatment warrants investigation for diabetes mellitus as it predisposes to persistent fungal and bacterial infections
balanitis.when_to_seek_help: See a doctor for balanitis not improving after 2 weeks of treatment, recurrent episodes, painful or difficult retraction of the foreskin (phimosis), penile ulcers or lumps, or any rapidly spreading redness or swelling suggesting cellulitis.

immediate_advice: Call emergency services immediately for cocaine-induced chest pain, seizure, stroke symptoms, or collapse — cocaine causes acute myocardial infarction even in young people with normal arteries
Move the person to a calm, quiet environment to reduce stimulation and agitation during acute intoxication
Do not administer beta-blockers for cocaine-related cardiovascular complications — they cause paradoxical vasoconstriction
quick_solutions: There is no approved pharmacotherapy specifically for cocaine use disorder — behavioral therapies are the primary treatment
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy and Contingency Management have the strongest evidence for cocaine use disorder
Medically supervised management of acute cardiovascular complications (hypertension, arrhythmia) in an emergency setting may require benzodiazepines and nitroglycerin
Treat co-occurring depression and anxiety which frequently underlie cocaine use disorder
drug abuse (cocaine).when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for chest pain (cocaine-induced MI), stroke symptoms, seizures, severe hypertension, hyperthermia, or respiratory failure. Seek urgent addiction medicine assessment for anyone wishing to stop cocaine use safely.
drug abuse (cocaine).specialist_recommendation: Addiction Medicine Specialist / Emergency Physician / Cardiologist (for cardiac complications)

immediate_advice: Do not attempt alcohol withdrawal without medical supervision — seizures and delirium tremens can be fatal
Seek urgent medical assessment immediately before stopping alcohol in a dependent individual
If withdrawal symptoms have already started (sweating, tremor, anxiety, racing heart), call emergency services or attend the emergency department immediately
quick_solutions: Medically supervised detoxification using a chlordiazepoxide reducing regimen (or diazepam) is the safe standard of care
Thiamine supplementation (oral or IV) must be given before glucose administration to prevent Wernicke's encephalopathy
IV fluids and electrolyte replacement address dehydration and hypomagnesemia which worsen seizure risk
Anti-seizure medications are used if seizures occur and benzodiazepines are the first-line treatment for alcohol withdrawal seizures
alcohol withdrawal.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for seizures, severe confusion or hallucinations (delirium tremens), fever, rapid heart rate, profuse sweating, or any loss of consciousness during alcohol withdrawal — this is a medical emergency with significant mortality.

immediate_advice: Reduce or eliminate caffeine, alcohol, nicotine, and energy drinks — these are the most common triggers for PACs
Ensure adequate sleep and manage stress — both sleep deprivation and psychological stress reliably increase PAC frequency
Avoid decongestant medications containing pseudoephedrine or phenylephrine which can provoke PACs
quick_solutions: Most PACs in structurally normal hearts are benign and require only reassurance and lifestyle modification
A 24-hour Holter monitor quantifies PAC burden and identifies any degeneration into atrial fibrillation or flutter
Echocardiogram is recommended to exclude structural heart disease — PACs in the context of cardiomyopathy or valvular disease require specialist management
Beta-blockers reduce PAC frequency and associated symptoms for patients significantly troubled by palpitations
premature atrial contractions (pacs).when_to_seek_help: Consult a cardiologist for very frequent PACs (more than 10,000 per day on Holter), PACs associated with syncope or near-syncope, known structural heart disease, or if PACs are triggering runs of atrial fibrillation.

immediate_advice: Call emergency services immediately for new onset hemiplegia — this is a neurological emergency indicating stroke or intracranial hemorrhage until proven otherwise
Note the exact time of symptom onset — this is critical for thrombolysis eligibility
Keep the person safe from falling and do not give food or water as swallowing may be impaired
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — urgent brain imaging, stroke unit admission, and thrombolysis or thrombectomy assessment are time-critical
Early rehabilitation with physiotherapy, occupational therapy, and speech therapy within 24–48 hours of stabilization improves functional recovery
Spasticity management (stretching, botulinum toxin, baclofen) is an important ongoing component of hemiplegia rehabilitation
Secondary stroke prevention (antiplatelet therapy, anticoagulation for AF, statin, antihypertensive) must be initiated before hospital discharge
hemiplegia.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for any sudden onset weakness or paralysis affecting one side of the body, face, arm, or leg — every minute without treatment results in irreversible neurological damage.
hemiplegia.specialist_recommendation: Neurologist / Stroke Rehabilitation Team (Emergency services first)

immediate_advice: Rest the affected joint and elevate it above heart level to reduce swelling
Apply ice wrapped in a cloth for 20 minutes every 2–3 hours during the first 48 hours
Seek urgent evaluation if the joint is hot, red, and very painful — septic arthritis is a surgical emergency
quick_solutions: Aspiration (arthrocentesis) of the joint is both diagnostic and therapeutic — fluid analysis identifies the cause (infection, gout, blood, inflammatory arthritis)
Septic arthritis requires emergency joint washout and IV antibiotics — delay causes rapid and irreversible cartilage destruction
NSAIDs and rest are appropriate for non-infectious effusions (post-traumatic, osteoarthritis, reactive arthritis)
Intra-articular corticosteroid injection is effective for inflammatory (non-infectious) effusions not responding to NSAIDs
joint effusion.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for a hot, swollen, exquisitely tender joint with fever — this is septic arthritis until proven otherwise and requires same-day aspiration and treatment. Also seek urgent care for post-traumatic hemarthrosis (blood in joint) which may indicate ligament rupture.

immediate_advice: Seek urgent neurological evaluation for any new persistent headache that is worse in the morning, associated with vomiting, or waking you from sleep
Take prescribed anti-seizure medications without omission if seizures have occurred
Do not drive if seizures have occurred within the legally mandated period in your jurisdiction
quick_solutions: Urgent MRI brain with contrast is the primary investigation and must not be delayed
Dexamethasone (corticosteroid) is frequently prescribed to reduce perilesional edema and relieve raised intracranial pressure symptoms
Treatment depends on histology and location: surgical resection, radiotherapy, chemotherapy (temozolomide for glioblastoma), or targeted therapy
Multidisciplinary team including neurosurgery, neuro-oncology, radiation oncology, and palliative care is essential from diagnosis
brain cancer.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for sudden severe headache, new seizure, rapidly progressive neurological deficit, confusion, loss of consciousness, or any symptoms suggesting raised intracranial pressure (morning headache with vomiting).

immediate_advice: Monitor pollen forecasts and limit outdoor activities on high-pollen days, particularly in the morning when counts peak
Keep windows and doors closed during high-pollen periods and use air conditioning where possible
Shower and change clothes after outdoor activity to remove pollen from hair and skin
quick_solutions: Intranasal corticosteroid sprays (fluticasone, mometasone) are the most effective treatment for nasal symptoms — begin 2 weeks before pollen season starts
Non-sedating antihistamines (cetirizine, loratadine, fexofenadine) control sneezing, itching, and watery eyes effectively
Allergen immunotherapy (injections or sublingual drops) over 3–5 years provides long-lasting reduction in allergy severity
Combination of nasal steroid spray plus antihistamine provides superior symptom control compared to either alone for moderate-to-severe hay fever
seasonal allergies (hay fever).when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for symptoms significantly affecting quality of life, work, or sleep despite OTC treatments, for associated asthma worsening during pollen season, or for formal allergy testing and immunotherapy consideration.

immediate_advice: Use preservative-free artificial tear eye drops frequently throughout the day — at least every 2–4 hours initially
Avoid environments with air conditioning, heating vents, fans, or low humidity directed at the face
Take regular screen breaks using the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds to restore natural blink rate
quick_solutions: Preservative-free lubricating eye drops (hyaluronic acid-based) are preferred for frequent use — preservatives in multi-dose drops cause ocular surface toxicity with prolonged use
Omega-3 fatty acid supplementation (fish oil or flaxseed oil) improves meibomian gland function and tear quality over 3 months
Warm eyelid compresses for 10 minutes twice daily improve the meibomian oil layer which prevents tear evaporation
Punctal plugs (inserted by an ophthalmologist) conserve tears by blocking lacrimal drainage and provide excellent relief for moderate-to-severe dry eye
dry eye of unknown cause.when_to_seek_help: Consult an ophthalmologist for dry eye causing significant visual disturbance, pain, photophobia, or not responding to lubricating drops after 4–6 weeks. Severe or progressive dry eye may indicate an underlying autoimmune condition such as Sjögren's syndrome.

immediate_advice: Do not share combs, brushes, hats, towels, or pillowcases — tinea capitis (scalp ringworm) is highly contagious
Avoid scratching the scalp as this spreads the infection and increases secondary bacterial infection risk
Wash bedding, towels, and headwear at high temperature to eliminate fungal spores
quick_solutions: Oral antifungal medication (griseofulvin or terbinafine) is required as topical treatments do not penetrate the hair shaft sufficiently
Antifungal shampoo (ketoconazole or selenium sulfide) is used as an adjunct to oral treatment and reduces spore shedding to prevent spread to household contacts
Household contacts and classmates should be screened and treated if asymptomatic carriage is confirmed
Treatment duration is typically 6–8 weeks — complete the full course even if symptoms resolve early
fungal infection of the hair.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for any scaly, itchy scalp rash with hair loss in a child, a fluctuant swelling on the scalp (kerion — requires urgent treatment to prevent scarring alopecia), or any scalp infection not responding to treatment.

immediate_advice: Take paracetamol and ibuprofen together (if no contraindications) for pain relief while awaiting dental treatment
Rinse gently with warm salt water several times daily to draw out pus and reduce bacterial load
Do not apply aspirin directly to the gum or tooth — this causes chemical burns to the mucosa
quick_solutions: Seek urgent dental care within 24–48 hours — a tooth abscess requires drainage (root canal treatment or extraction) and antibiotics
Antibiotics (amoxicillin or metronidazole) are prescribed when spreading infection or systemic features are present
Do not delay dental treatment hoping the abscess will resolve — dental abscesses do not resolve without drainage
After definitive treatment, address the underlying cause (caries, failed root canal, periodontal disease) to prevent recurrence
tooth abscess.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for facial swelling extending to the eye or neck, difficulty swallowing or breathing (Ludwig's angina — life-threatening submandibular space infection), fever above 38.5°C, or rapidly spreading facial cellulitis.
tooth abscess.specialist_recommendation: Dentist / Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeon (for spreading infections)

immediate_advice: Call emergency services or a poison control center immediately for any bite from a venomous species, severe local reaction, or systemic symptoms
Keep the affected limb immobile and at heart level — do not apply a tourniquet or attempt to suck out venom
Apply a pressure immobilization bandage for neurotoxic snake or funnel-web spider bites if trained to do so
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — antivenom may be required and must be administered in a hospital setting with resuscitation capability
Wash the bite site thoroughly with soap and water to reduce infection risk
Tetanus prophylaxis should be updated if immunization status is uncertain
Ice packs may reduce local pain from insect stings but are not recommended for snake bites as they can cause tissue damage
envenomation from spider or animal bite.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for severe pain, rapidly spreading local reaction, systemic symptoms (nausea, vomiting, muscle weakness, difficulty breathing, dizziness, palpitations), or any bite from an unidentified or potentially venomous creature.
envenomation from spider or animal bite.specialist_recommendation: Emergency Physician / Toxicologist / Infectious Disease Specialist

immediate_advice: Avoid prolonged sitting on the toilet and straining at stool — these worsen most anorectal conditions
Increase dietary fiber and fluid intake to soften stools and reduce the trauma of defecation
Take warm sitz baths for 10–15 minutes after bowel movements to relieve pain and promote healing
quick_solutions: Topical anesthetic and anti-inflammatory preparations (lidocaine, hydrocortisone) provide symptomatic relief for perianal pain and inflammation
Stool softeners (docusate) or osmotic laxatives (lactulose) maintain soft stool consistency and minimize straining
Proctoscopy or sigmoidoscopy is often required to directly visualize and diagnose the specific rectal pathology
Specific treatment depends on diagnosis: hemorrhoids, fissure, fistula, prolapse, and colorectal cancer all require distinct management approaches
rectal disorder.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent evaluation for rectal bleeding (especially in adults over 40), change in bowel habit lasting more than 4 weeks, rectal pain severe enough to prevent sitting, prolapse of tissue through the anus, or any mucus or pus in the stool.

immediate_advice: Sit or lie down immediately when vertigo occurs to prevent falls
Move slowly and deliberately when changing head position — avoid rapid head movements
Do not drive during active vertigo episodes
quick_solutions: The Epley maneuver (canalith repositioning procedure) performed by a physiotherapist or doctor resolves BPPV in over 80% of patients — often in a single session
Brandt-Daroff exercises performed at home can be taught to patients for self-management between appointments
Vestibular suppressant medications (prochlorperazine, meclizine) provide short-term relief but do not treat the underlying cause and may delay recovery
Recurrence is common — patients should learn to identify the triggering position and perform home Epley exercises when symptoms return
benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (bp.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent neurological evaluation for vertigo associated with severe headache, double vision, difficulty swallowing, limb weakness or numbness, inability to walk, or hearing loss — these suggest a central cause requiring brain imaging.

immediate_advice: Avoid direct sunlight exposure and do not take vitamin D supplements without medical supervision — sarcoidosis causes hypercalcemia which is worsened by sun exposure and vitamin D
Do not smoke — smoking worsens pulmonary sarcoidosis and impairs corticosteroid treatment response
Seek urgent evaluation for breathlessness, chest pain, significant visual disturbance, or confusion
quick_solutions: Many cases of pulmonary sarcoidosis (Stage I and II) remit spontaneously — watchful waiting with monitoring is appropriate for asymptomatic or mildly symptomatic disease
Oral corticosteroids (prednisolone) are the mainstay treatment for symptomatic pulmonary, cardiac, neurological, or ocular sarcoidosis
Steroid-sparing agents (methotrexate, azathioprine, hydroxychloroquine) are used for chronic or steroid-dependent disease
Regular monitoring of lung function, calcium levels, ACE levels, ECG, and ophthalmological review are essential
sarcoidosis.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent care for rapidly worsening breathlessness, visual loss or eye pain, palpitations or syncope (cardiac sarcoidosis), facial nerve palsy, or hypercalcemia symptoms (nausea, confusion, excessive thirst).

immediate_advice: Apply a warm compress to the closed eyelid for 10–15 minutes, 3–4 times daily — this is the primary and most effective treatment
Do not squeeze or attempt to puncture the stye — this risks spreading infection
Do not wear contact lenses or eye makeup until the stye has fully resolved
quick_solutions: Most styes resolve spontaneously within 1–2 weeks with warm compress treatment alone
Topical antibiotic ointment (erythromycin or chloramphenicol) is sometimes prescribed to prevent secondary spread
Eyelid hygiene using diluted baby shampoo or commercially available eyelid wipes reduces recurrence
Persistent or very large styes may require incision and drainage by an ophthalmologist under local anesthetic
stye.when_to_seek_help: See a doctor if the stye does not begin improving after 2 weeks of warm compresses, if the entire eyelid becomes significantly swollen and red (preseptal cellulitis), if there is fever, or if vision is affected.

immediate_advice: Do not rub or apply pressure to the eyes
Avoid strenuous exercise and heavy lifting if floaters have developed acutely as this can increase intraocular pressure
Seek urgent ophthalmological review for any sudden significant increase in floaters or new flashing lights
quick_solutions: Most vitreous floaters from age-related posterior vitreous detachment (PVD) are benign and the brain gradually learns to ignore them over weeks to months
Urgent dilated fundus examination is required for new floaters or flashing lights to exclude retinal tear or detachment
Vitrectomy surgery can be offered for visually significant floaters significantly affecting quality of life, but carries surgical risks that must be carefully weighed
Laser vitreolysis is an alternative less invasive option for select cases of floaters near the visual axis
vitreous degeneration.when_to_seek_help: Seek same-day urgent ophthalmological evaluation for a sudden shower of new floaters, prominent flashing lights, a shadow or curtain in the visual field, or any sudden change in vision — these may indicate retinal tear or detachment.

immediate_advice: Apply a thick, fragrance-free emollient moisturizer immediately after bathing while skin is still slightly damp to lock in moisture
Avoid hot water, harsh soaps, and prolonged bathing — use lukewarm water and gentle fragrance-free cleansers only
Protect atrophic skin from trauma, pressure, and friction — even minor injuries can cause significant skin breakdown
quick_solutions: Frequent application of barrier creams and emollients is the cornerstone of management for atrophic skin
Topical retinoids (tretinoin) can partially restore skin thickness in photoaged or steroid-induced atrophy under dermatologist supervision
Review and reduce prolonged topical corticosteroid use if steroid-induced atrophy is the cause
Protect skin from UV radiation at all times with high SPF sunscreen and protective clothing as sun damage accelerates skin atrophy
atrophic skin condition.when_to_seek_help: Consult a dermatologist for rapidly progressive skin thinning, skin atrophy affecting large body surface areas, skin breakdown or ulceration developing on atrophic skin, or atrophy associated with systemic symptoms suggesting an autoimmune cause.

immediate_advice: Call emergency services or obstetric team immediately — uterine atony causes life-threatening postpartum hemorrhage
Uterine fundal massage performed by the midwife or obstetrician stimulates uterine contraction and reduces bleeding
Establish IV access and begin IV fluid resuscitation while awaiting definitive treatment
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — oxytocin infusion is the first-line uterotonic medication and must be administered promptly
Second-line uterotonics (ergometrine, carboprost, misoprostol) are used if oxytocin alone is insufficient
Uterine balloon tamponade, B-Lynch suture, or uterine artery embolization are escalating interventions for refractory hemorrhage
Emergency hysterectomy may be life-saving when all other measures fail
uterine atony.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency obstetric services immediately for heavy postpartum bleeding, rapid deterioration in maternal condition, or suspected uterine atony following delivery — this is a life-threatening obstetric emergency.
uterine atony.specialist_recommendation: Obstetrician / Interventional Radiologist / Critical Care (Emergency obstetric team first)

immediate_advice: Avoid spicy, acidic, and abrasive foods that irritate oral lesions
Maintain meticulous oral hygiene with a soft toothbrush and alcohol-free mouthwash
Do not use tobacco products in any form — tobacco is a major cause of oral cancer and significantly delays healing of benign lesions
quick_solutions: Topical corticosteroid paste (triamcinolone acetonide) or mouthwash reduces pain and speeds healing of aphthous ulcers
Topical anesthetic gels (benzocaine, lidocaine) provide effective temporary pain relief for symptomatic ulcers
Any oral lesion that does not heal within 3 weeks requires urgent biopsy to exclude oral cancer
Identify and address local causes: sharp tooth, ill-fitting denture, or habitual cheek biting
oral mucosal lesion.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent dental or medical evaluation for any oral lesion persisting beyond 3 weeks, any white or red patch (leukoplakia or erythroplakia), unexplained tooth loosening, persistent jaw pain, or a lump in the oral cavity — these are potential early signs of oral cancer.
oral mucosal lesion.specialist_recommendation: Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeon / Dentist / Otolaryngologist (ENT)

immediate_advice: Call emergency services immediately — this is a life-threatening neurological emergency
Keep the person calm and lying down with head elevated at approximately 30 degrees
Do not give food, water, or any medications by mouth
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — urgent CT brain imaging, blood pressure control, reversal of anticoagulation, and neurosurgical consultation are all time-critical
Blood pressure is aggressively lowered to target systolic below 140 mmHg to limit hematoma expansion
Surgical evacuation is considered for cerebellar hemorrhages or selected cases with significant mass effect
Early rehabilitation beginning within 24–48 hours of stabilization improves long-term functional outcomes
intracerebral hemorrhage.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for sudden severe headache, sudden onset weakness or speech difficulty, loss of consciousness, or seizure — every minute of delay worsens the extent of irreversible brain injury.
intracerebral hemorrhage.specialist_recommendation: Neurosurgeon / Neurologist / Critical Care (Emergency services first)

immediate_advice: Ensure the home environment is safe: remove trip hazards, install grab rails, use stove knob covers, and consider door alarms to prevent wandering
Maintain a consistent daily routine — predictability significantly reduces anxiety and behavioral disturbances in Alzheimer's disease
Do not leave the person alone if there are safety concerns — assess the need for supervised care early
quick_solutions: Cholinesterase inhibitors (donepezil, rivastigmine, galantamine) slow cognitive decline in mild-to-moderate disease and should be initiated early
Memantine is used for moderate-to-severe Alzheimer's disease, alone or in combination with cholinesterase inhibitors
Cognitive stimulation therapy and structured social activities maintain function and quality of life for longer
Carer support and education are essential — carer burnout is common and support organizations provide vital assistance
alzheimer disease.when_to_seek_help: Consult a geriatrician or neurologist for formal cognitive assessment if memory problems are affecting daily function. Seek urgent care for sudden acute confusion (delirium) in a person with known Alzheimer's — this usually indicates an intercurrent infection or medication issue.

immediate_advice: Most ganglion cysts are harmless and require no immediate action — observation is appropriate for asymptomatic cysts
Avoid repetitive wrist movements that irritate the cyst during symptomatic periods
Do not attempt to burst the cyst by striking it (the old 'Bible cyst' method) — this is painful, ineffective, and can damage surrounding structures
quick_solutions: Up to 50% of ganglion cysts resolve spontaneously within 1–2 years without any treatment
Aspiration (draining with a needle) by a doctor provides relief but has a higher recurrence rate than surgery
Surgical excision provides definitive treatment with lower recurrence rates than aspiration
Immobilization with a wrist splint can reduce pain and may slow cyst growth in symptomatic cases
ganglion cyst.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor if the cyst is causing significant pain, restricting joint movement, compressing a nerve (causing tingling or weakness), is rapidly enlarging, or if there is uncertainty about whether the lump is a simple cyst.
ganglion cyst.specialist_recommendation: Orthopedic Surgeon / Hand Surgeon / General Practitioner

immediate_advice: During a flare, rest and maintain adequate hydration — liquid nutrition (elemental or polymeric formula) may be better tolerated than solid foods
Do not stop prescribed immunosuppressive or biologic medications during a flare without consulting your gastroenterologist
Seek emergency care for severe abdominal pain, significant rectal bleeding, fever, or suspected obstruction or perforation
quick_solutions: Corticosteroids (prednisolone or budesonide) induce remission during active flares
Maintenance therapy with immunosuppressants (azathioprine, mercaptopurine) or biologics (infliximab, adalimumab) prevents relapse
Dietary adjustments (low-residue diet during flares, identifying personal food triggers) reduce symptom burden
Regular colonoscopic surveillance is required due to the increased risk of colorectal cancer associated with long-standing Crohn's disease
crohn disease.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for severe abdominal pain, fever above 38.5°C, significant rectal bleeding, signs of bowel obstruction (distension, vomiting, inability to pass gas or stool), or symptoms of fistula or abscess formation.

immediate_advice: Sit upright immediately — this reduces preload and relieves breathlessness from pulmonary edema
Call emergency services for severe sudden breathlessness, inability to lie flat, or oxygen saturation below 94%
Restrict fluid and sodium intake immediately pending medical evaluation
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately for acute pulmonary edema — IV furosemide, oxygen, and hemodynamic monitoring are standard initial interventions
Identify and treat the underlying cause: heart failure, renal failure, hepatic cirrhosis, or iatrogenic over-infusion
Daily weight monitoring is the most sensitive early warning sign — report a weight gain of more than 2kg in 48 hours to your doctor
Strict sodium restriction (less than 2g per day) and diuretic titration manage chronic fluid overload in heart failure and cirrhosis
fluid overload.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for sudden severe breathlessness, pink frothy sputum, inability to lie flat, peripheral edema extending above the knee and worsening rapidly, or oxygen saturation below 94%.

immediate_advice: Seek urgent medical evaluation — atrial flutter with rapid ventricular rate can cause hemodynamic instability
Take all prescribed rate-control or antiarrhythmic medications consistently
Avoid caffeine, alcohol, stimulant medications, and significant physical exertion pending specialist review
quick_solutions: Rate control with beta-blockers or calcium channel blockers (diltiazem, verapamil) is the immediate priority for rapid atrial flutter
Electrical cardioversion is highly effective for restoring sinus rhythm in hemodynamically stable flutter
Catheter ablation of the cavotricuspid isthmus is curative for typical (type I) atrial flutter with very high success rates
Anticoagulation assessment using CHA2DS2-VASc score is mandatory — stroke risk management parallels that of atrial fibrillation
atrial flutter.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for palpitations with chest pain, breathlessness, dizziness, near-syncope, or syncope — these indicate hemodynamically significant atrial flutter requiring urgent cardioversion or rate control.

immediate_advice: Avoid scratching and keep the skin clean and dry
Apply fragrance-free emollient moisturizer generously to affected areas
Avoid potential irritants: harsh soaps, synthetic fabrics, extreme temperatures, and known personal triggers
quick_solutions: Document the appearance, distribution, timeline, and any associated symptoms to help your dermatologist make an accurate diagnosis
Avoid self-treating with topical steroids without medical advice — inappropriate steroid use can mask serious diagnoses and cause skin thinning
Patch testing may be required to identify contact allergens causing the skin disorder
Biopsy is often necessary for definitive diagnosis of chronic, atypical, or treatment-resistant skin conditions
skin disorder.when_to_seek_help: Consult a dermatologist promptly for rapidly spreading rashes, blistering or ulceration, skin changes associated with systemic symptoms (fever, joint pain, fatigue), any pigmented lesion changing in size, shape, or color, or any skin condition not responding to 2 weeks of basic self-care.

immediate_advice: For acute tooth pain: take paracetamol and ibuprofen together (if no contraindications) and seek same-day or next-day dental review
For a knocked-out adult tooth: handle only by the crown, rinse gently without scrubbing, replace in the socket immediately if possible, or store in milk — seek emergency dental care within 30 minutes
Do not apply aspirin directly to tooth or gum tissue
quick_solutions: See a dentist promptly — most tooth disorders worsen with delay and early intervention is always more conservative and less expensive
Maintain twice-daily brushing and daily flossing to prevent the most common tooth disorders (caries and periodontal disease)
Fluoride mouthwash used daily after brushing significantly reduces caries risk
A custom nightguard from your dentist protects teeth from bruxism-related wear and fracture
tooth disorder.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency dental care for severe uncontrolled tooth pain, swelling of the face or neck, fever with dental pain, a knocked-out permanent tooth, or a fractured tooth with exposed pulp.

immediate_advice: Call emergency services immediately — do not drive yourself to the hospital
Chew a single adult aspirin (300mg) immediately if not allergic and not already taking anticoagulants
Sit or lie in the most comfortable position, loosen tight clothing, and keep calm while waiting for emergency services
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — primary percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) must be performed within 90 minutes of presentation for optimal outcomes
Thrombolytic therapy is an alternative when PCI is not available within 120 minutes of diagnosis
Dual antiplatelet therapy, statins, beta-blockers, and ACE inhibitors are initiated in hospital and continued long-term
Cardiac rehabilitation after discharge significantly reduces mortality and recurrence risk — enroll without delay
heart attack.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for central crushing chest pain or pressure, pain radiating to the arm, jaw, or back, sweating, nausea, breathlessness, or a sense of impending doom — every minute of delay results in irreversible myocardial damage.

immediate_advice: Do not attempt to walk on the injured leg if fracture is suspected — immobilize in the position found
Apply ice wrapped in cloth to reduce swelling and apply a makeshift splint if trained to do so
Seek emergency care or call emergency services for significant leg injury with deformity
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately for formal X-ray, reduction if displaced, and casting or surgical fixation
Elevation of the limb reduces post-fracture edema
Weight-bearing status, duration of immobilization, and physiotherapy requirements depend on fracture type, location, and treatment method
Calcium and vitamin D supplementation supports bone healing throughout the recovery period
fracture of the leg.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for any suspected leg fracture, especially open fractures (bone visible through skin), absent pulse in the foot, significant deformity, rapidly expanding compartment (tight, painful, pale limb suggesting compartment syndrome), or inability to move toes.

immediate_advice: Rinse the mouth with warm salt water after meals to reduce fungal load
Remove dentures at night, clean them thoroughly, and soak in an antifungal solution — dentures are a major reservoir for Candida
Avoid sugary foods and drinks which promote Candida overgrowth
quick_solutions: Topical antifungal treatment with nystatin oral suspension (swish and swallow 4 times daily for 7–14 days) is first-line treatment
Miconazole oromucosal gel is an effective alternative, particularly for denture-related thrush
Oral fluconazole (150mg single dose or 50mg daily for 7 days) is used for moderate-to-severe or refractory cases
Investigate for predisposing causes: inhaled corticosteroid use (rinse mouth after each use), antibiotic use, immunosuppression, poorly controlled diabetes, or HIV
oral thrush (yeast infection).when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for thrush not responding to topical treatment after 2 weeks, recurrent episodes (more than 4 per year), thrush spreading to the esophagus (causing painful swallowing), or thrush in an immunocompromised individual.
oral thrush (yeast infection).specialist_recommendation: General Practitioner / Dentist / Infectious Disease Specialist (for immunocompromised patients)

immediate_advice: Rise slowly from lying to sitting, pause for 30 seconds, then from sitting to standing to allow blood pressure to equilibrate
Sit or lie down immediately when feeling dizzy after standing to prevent a fall and injury
Avoid prolonged standing in hot environments, hot baths or showers, and alcohol which all worsen orthostatic hypotension
quick_solutions: Increase fluid and salt intake (unless contraindicated by heart failure or hypertension) to expand intravascular volume
Wear medical-grade compression stockings (at least 30–40 mmHg) to reduce venous pooling in the legs
Review all medications with your doctor — antihypertensives, diuretics, tricyclic antidepressants, alpha-blockers, and dopamine agonists are common causes
Fludrocortisone or midodrine may be prescribed for persistent symptomatic orthostatic hypotension
orthostatic hypotension.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor for recurrent episodes of dizziness on standing, near-fainting, or falls. Seek urgent evaluation for syncope (actual loss of consciousness on standing), orthostatic hypotension in the context of Parkinson's disease, or if a new medication change preceded symptoms.

immediate_advice: Sleep on your side rather than your back — the lateral position significantly reduces airway collapse and apnea frequency
Avoid alcohol within 4 hours of bedtime — alcohol relaxes upper airway muscles and worsens OSA significantly
Avoid sedative medications (benzodiazepines, opioids) without specific medical guidance as they suppress respiratory drive
quick_solutions: Continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) therapy is the most effective treatment for moderate-to-severe OSA and eliminates apneas in virtually all patients
Weight loss of even 10% of body weight significantly reduces OSA severity and may resolve it in mild cases
Mandibular advancement device (MAD) fitted by a dentist is an effective alternative for mild-to-moderate OSA or CPAP-intolerant patients
Upper airway surgery is reserved for specific anatomical causes (enlarged tonsils, nasal obstruction, significant maxillomandibular abnormalities)
obstructive sleep apnea (osa).when_to_seek_help: Consult a sleep medicine specialist or respiratory physician for loud snoring with witnessed apneas, excessive daytime sleepiness affecting driving or work safety, morning headaches, or poor concentration attributable to sleep disruption.

immediate_advice: Seek emergency evaluation for severe hypokalemia (potassium below 2.5 mmol/L) — this can cause life-threatening cardiac arrhythmias and muscle paralysis
Avoid strenuous exercise until potassium is corrected — the cardiac risk is significantly elevated during physical exertion
Take prescribed oral potassium supplements exactly as directed — do not crush extended-release formulations
quick_solutions: Oral potassium chloride supplementation is appropriate for mild-to-moderate hypokalemia — high-potassium foods (banana, avocado, orange juice, potatoes) are adjunctive but insufficient alone for significant deficiency
IV potassium replacement is required for severe hypokalemia or if oral route is not feasible — this must always be done in hospital with cardiac monitoring
Correct concurrent hypomagnesemia — low magnesium prevents effective potassium repletion and must be treated simultaneously
Identify and address the underlying cause: diuretics, vomiting, diarrhea, hyperaldosteronism, or inadequate dietary intake
hypokalemia.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for muscle weakness, cramps, paralysis, palpitations, or ECG changes — severe hypokalemia is a cardiac emergency. Seek urgent evaluation for potassium below 3.0 mmol/L on blood tests even if asymptomatic.

immediate_advice: Moisturize affected skin regularly with thick, fragrance-free emollients to reduce scaling, itching, and fissuring
Avoid triggers: stress, smoking, alcohol, certain medications (beta-blockers, lithium, antimalarials), and skin trauma (Koebner phenomenon)
Use gentle, non-irritating skincare products and avoid picking or scratching plaques which can worsen lesions
quick_solutions: Topical treatments are first-line: potent corticosteroids (betamethasone), vitamin D analogues (calcipotriol), or their combination (Dovobet) are highly effective for plaque psoriasis
Phototherapy (narrowband UVB) is highly effective for widespread psoriasis and is a safe long-term option
Methotrexate, ciclosporin, or acitretin are systemic treatments for moderate-to-severe psoriasis not responding to topicals
Biologic therapies (TNF inhibitors, IL-17, IL-23 inhibitors) provide excellent and sustained clearance for severe psoriasis
psoriasis.when_to_seek_help: Consult a dermatologist for psoriasis affecting more than 10% of body surface area, psoriasis involving the face, hands, feet, or genitals, associated joint pain (psoriatic arthritis), or disease significantly affecting quality of life, work, or mental health.

immediate_advice: Avoid sexual intercourse until the cause has been identified and treatment has been completed
Ensure sexual partners are informed and tested — most infectious causes of cervicitis are sexually transmitted
Do not douche or use vaginal deodorants which alter the vaginal environment and worsen cervical inflammation
quick_solutions: Empirical treatment covering both Chlamydia trachomatis (azithromycin or doxycycline) and Neisseria gonorrhoeae (ceftriaxone) is often initiated before culture results are available
Complete the full antibiotic course and abstain from intercourse until both patient and partner(s) have completed treatment
STI screening (HIV, syphilis, hepatitis B) should be offered to all patients with infectious cervicitis
All sexual contacts within the preceding 60 days require notification, testing, and treatment
cervicitis.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor promptly for any cervical discharge, intermenstrual or post-coital bleeding, pelvic pain, or pain during intercourse — early treatment prevents progression to pelvic inflammatory disease, which can cause infertility.
cervicitis.specialist_recommendation: Gynecologist / Sexual Health Specialist / General Practitioner

immediate_advice: Seek emergency ENT or emergency care immediately — pharyngeal abscesses can compromise the airway rapidly
Do not take food or drink while awaiting surgical assessment
Monitor breathing continuously — any stridor, drooling, or difficulty managing secretions indicates imminent airway compromise
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — CT imaging of the neck is required to delineate the abscess extent and plan surgical drainage
IV antibiotics (broad-spectrum covering anaerobes) are initiated immediately in hospital
Surgical drainage under general anesthesia is the definitive treatment in almost all cases
Intensive care monitoring post-drainage is required for large abscesses with significant airway involvement
abscess of the pharynx.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services or attend the emergency department immediately for severe throat pain with drooling, muffled voice, difficulty opening the mouth, stridor, difficulty breathing, fever with neck stiffness, or rapidly increasing neck swelling.

immediate_advice: Use a walking stick in the opposite hand to the affected hip to offload the joint and reduce pain
Apply heat to the hip for 15–20 minutes before activity to reduce stiffness and pain
Avoid high-impact activities (running, jumping) that load the joint — switch to low-impact alternatives
quick_solutions: Regular low-impact exercise (swimming, cycling, walking in a pool) maintains muscle strength and joint mobility without excessive loading
Weight loss reduces hip joint loading by 3–6 times the weight lost — even modest weight reduction provides significant symptom relief
NSAIDs are first-line pharmacological treatment for pain and inflammation
Total hip replacement is a highly effective, durable surgical intervention for end-stage hip arthritis significantly limiting function
arthritis of the hip.when_to_seek_help: Consult an orthopedic surgeon when hip pain is significantly affecting walking distance, sleep, daily activities, or quality of life despite adequate conservative management — the timing of joint replacement surgery balances symptom severity against surgical risk.

immediate_advice: Seek urgent audiological evaluation for any sudden sensorineural hearing loss — treatment within 72 hours significantly improves outcomes
Avoid loud noise exposure immediately and use hearing protection in all noisy environments going forward
Do not insert objects into the ear canal as this cannot address sensorineural causes
quick_solutions: Sudden sensorineural hearing loss is treated with high-dose oral or intratympanic corticosteroids — this is a medical emergency requiring same-day treatment
Hearing aids are highly effective for stable sensorineural hearing loss and significantly improve communication and quality of life
Cochlear implants are recommended for severe-to-profound bilateral sensorineural hearing loss not benefiting from conventional hearing aids
Protect remaining hearing rigorously: avoid occupational and recreational noise, use custom ear protection, and avoid ototoxic medications where possible
sensorineural hearing loss.when_to_seek_help: Seek same-day urgent ENT evaluation for any sudden hearing loss in one or both ears — this is a treatable emergency when caught early. Also seek evaluation for asymmetric hearing loss, hearing loss with tinnitus or vertigo, or progressive hearing loss over months.

immediate_advice: Fall-proof the home environment immediately: remove loose rugs, install grab rails in bathroom, improve lighting, and wear non-slip footwear
Avoid activities with a high fall risk until bone density and balance have been assessed and optimized
Ensure adequate calcium intake through diet (dairy, fortified foods, leafy greens) — supplements are adjunctive, not a substitute for dietary calcium
quick_solutions: Bisphosphonates (alendronate, risedronate) are first-line pharmacological treatment and significantly reduce fracture risk
Calcium (1000–1200mg daily) and vitamin D (800–1000 IU daily) supplementation is recommended for all patients on osteoporosis treatment
Weight-bearing and resistance exercise (walking, dancing, weight training) maintains bone density and improves balance and muscle strength
DEXA scan at baseline and every 2 years monitors treatment response and guides duration of bisphosphonate therapy
osteoporosis.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for any fall causing significant pain as this may indicate fracture — vertebral fractures can occur with minimal trauma. Consult a doctor for height loss of more than 4cm, new onset back pain after minor activity, or a first fragility fracture.

immediate_advice: Seek urgent medical evaluation — ileus causes bowel obstruction symptoms and can lead to serious complications if untreated
Do not eat or drink anything until medically assessed
Report the time of last bowel movement and any abdominal surgery to emergency staff
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — nasogastric decompression, IV fluid replacement, and correction of electrolyte abnormalities (potassium, magnesium) are the initial treatments
Identify and treat the underlying cause: post-operative state, hypokalemia, opioid medications, peritonitis, or sepsis
Alvimopan (a peripherally acting opioid antagonist) is used post-operatively to accelerate return of bowel function
Early ambulation after surgery is the single most effective preventive measure against post-operative ileus
ileus.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for inability to pass gas or stool, significant abdominal distension, persistent nausea and vomiting, or abdominal pain — particularly in the post-operative setting or after abdominal injury.

immediate_advice: Seek emergency care immediately for a pain crisis — hospital management with IV analgesia, hydration, and oxygen is required
Stay warm, avoid cold temperatures, and maintain adequate hydration at all times to prevent sickling
Do not delay seeking care — untreated sickle cell crisis can cause acute chest syndrome, stroke, or organ failure
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — IV opioid analgesia, aggressive IV hydration, supplemental oxygen, and blood transfusion may all be required
Hydroxyurea therapy significantly reduces the frequency and severity of pain crises and should be discussed with your hematologist
Ensure vaccinations are current: pneumococcal, meningococcal, Haemophilus influenzae type b, and annual influenza vaccines are mandatory
Penicillin prophylaxis is prescribed lifelong for children with sickle cell disease to prevent overwhelming bacterial sepsis
sickle cell crisis.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for severe pain crisis, fever above 38.5°C, chest pain or breathlessness (acute chest syndrome), sudden neurological symptoms (stroke), severe anemia, or priapism lasting more than 2 hours.

immediate_advice: Avoid sexual intercourse until the cause has been identified and both you and your partner(s) have completed treatment
Drink plenty of water to maintain urinary flow and reduce urethral irritation
Do not self-treat with leftover antibiotics — gonococcal and non-gonococcal urethritis require different treatment regimens
quick_solutions: Empirical antibiotic treatment covering both Chlamydia (azithromycin or doxycycline) and Gonorrhea (ceftriaxone IM) is initiated pending culture results
Ensure sexual partner(s) from the preceding 60 days are notified, tested, and treated
Complete the full antibiotic course and abstain from intercourse for 7 days after single-dose treatment or until course completion
Screen for co-infections: HIV, syphilis, and hepatitis B/C testing should be offered at diagnosis
urethritis.when_to_seek_help: Consult a doctor promptly for urethral discharge, burning on urination, or urethral discomfort — early treatment prevents ascending infection (epididymitis, prostatitis) and partner transmission.
urethritis.specialist_recommendation: Sexual Health Specialist / Urologist / General Practitioner

immediate_advice: Seek emergency care immediately for acute bacterial prostatitis with high fever, rigors, perineal pain, and urinary retention — this can progress to urosepsis rapidly
Stay well hydrated to maintain urinary flow
Avoid prolonged sitting on hard surfaces and bicycle riding which compress the prostate and worsen symptoms
quick_solutions: Acute bacterial prostatitis requires IV antibiotics in hospital followed by 4–6 weeks of oral fluoroquinolones (ciprofloxacin) or trimethoprim
Alpha-blockers (tamsulosin) relieve urinary obstruction symptoms and are beneficial in both acute and chronic prostatitis
Chronic prostatitis/chronic pelvic pain syndrome is managed with a multimodal approach: antibiotics (if bacterial), alpha-blockers, pelvic floor physiotherapy, and pain management
Warm sitz baths provide symptomatic relief for perineal and pelvic pain
prostatitis.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for acute prostatitis with high fever, severe perineal pain, urinary retention, or signs of sepsis. Consult a urologist for chronic pelvic pain, recurrent urinary tract infections, or symptoms persisting beyond 3 months.

immediate_advice: Keep the ear completely dry — use a cotton wool ball coated in petroleum jelly to protect the ear during showering
Do not insert cotton buds, fingers, or any objects into the ear canal as this worsens inflammation and introduces further bacteria
Tilt the head to let water drain out after swimming and use a hairdryer on the lowest heat setting at arm's length to dry the ear canal
quick_solutions: Topical antibiotic-steroid ear drops (ciprofloxacin-dexamethasone or acetic acid preparations) are the primary treatment — apply as prescribed for 7–10 days
An ear wick may be inserted by a clinician if the canal is significantly swollen, to ensure drops reach the deeper canal
Acetic acid (2% white vinegar) ear drops used after swimming effectively prevent recurrence by restoring the canal's natural acidic environment
Use custom-fitted silicone ear plugs for swimming once the infection has resolved to prevent future episodes
otitis externa (swimmer's ear).when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent care for spreading redness and swelling of the outer ear and face (malignant otitis externa — especially dangerous in diabetics and immunocompromised patients), severe pain, fever, or facial nerve weakness.

immediate_advice: Seek urgent urological evaluation for acute urinary retention caused by urethral stricture — catheterization may be required urgently
Do not strain forcefully to void as this can cause urethral bleeding and worsen discomfort
Monitor for fever with urinary symptoms which may indicate a urinary tract infection requiring antibiotics
quick_solutions: Urethral dilation or optical internal urethrotomy provides effective short-term relief but has high recurrence rates for longer strictures
Urethroplasty (surgical reconstruction) offers the best long-term cure rates for significant or recurrent strictures
Clean intermittent self-catheterization (CISC) is taught as a temporizing measure to maintain urinary flow in select patients
Identify and treat the underlying cause: previous catheterization trauma, STI (gonorrhea), urethral injury, or lichen sclerosus
urethral stricture.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for complete inability to urinate (acute urinary retention), fever with urinary symptoms, or significant blood in urine. Consult a urologist for any weakened urinary stream, straining to void, or recurrent urinary tract infections in a male.

immediate_advice: Apply heat to the neck and shoulder muscles for 15–20 minutes to relieve muscle spasm and stiffness
Maintain gentle range-of-motion movements — complete immobilization worsens stiffness and delays recovery
Use a supportive cervical pillow to maintain neutral neck alignment during sleep
quick_solutions: NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen) and muscle relaxants are effective for acute neck pain with significant muscle spasm
Physiotherapy with manual therapy, cervical mobilization, and postural re-education provides the best long-term outcomes
Ergonomic adjustment of workstation and screen height significantly reduces ongoing cervical muscle strain
Cervical epidural steroid injection is appropriate for neck pain with associated radiculopathy (arm pain, numbness, or weakness) not responding to physiotherapy
pain disorder affecting the neck.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for neck pain following significant trauma (rule out fracture or instability before any movement). Seek urgent evaluation for neck pain with fever and stiffness (meningitis), pain radiating down both arms with leg weakness (myelopathy), or progressive neurological deficits.

immediate_advice: Keep the infant in an upright position to ease breathing
Ensure adequate fluid intake — breastfeeding or bottle feeding more frequently than usual maintains hydration in infants
Seek emergency care immediately for fast or labored breathing, nasal flaring, rib retractions, blue lips, or if the infant is not feeding
quick_solutions: Bronchiolitis is most commonly caused by RSV — antibiotics are not effective and not indicated
Nasal saline drops followed by gentle nasal suctioning clears secretions and improves feeding and breathing
Paracetamol or ibuprofen (age-appropriate dosing) relieves fever and discomfort
Bronchodilators and corticosteroids are not routinely recommended in bronchiolitis as evidence does not support their use
acute bronchiolitis.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for breathing rate above 60 per minute in an infant, nasal flaring, significant chest retractions, grunting, central cyanosis (blue lips or tongue), or poor feeding with fewer than half normal feeds for 24 hours.

immediate_advice: Avoid straining to read in poor lighting — ensure adequate bright, even illumination for close work
Hold reading material slightly further away at arm's length as a temporary measure if reading glasses are not yet available
Schedule an optometrist appointment for refraction testing and reading glasses or progressive lens prescription
quick_solutions: Reading glasses (single vision) are the simplest and most cost-effective correction for presbyopia
Progressive addition lenses (PALs) correct both distance and near vision in a single lens — require an adaptation period of 1–2 weeks
Multifocal contact lenses or monovision contact lens correction are alternatives for those preferring not to wear glasses
Refractive surgery options (LASIK monovision, corneal inlays, refractive lens exchange) are available for suitable candidates — discuss with an ophthalmologist
presbyopia.when_to_seek_help: Consult an optometrist for a current prescription update if existing reading glasses are no longer adequate, or if blurred near vision is causing eye strain, headaches, or difficulty with daily tasks.

immediate_advice: Ensure the home environment is safe: remove fall hazards, install stove guards, secure medications, and use door alarms to prevent wandering
Maintain consistent daily routines — familiarity and predictability significantly reduce agitation and distress
Do not leave the person alone if safety is a concern — assess supervision needs and plan accordingly
quick_solutions: Cholinesterase inhibitors (donepezil, rivastigmine) slow cognitive decline in Alzheimer's and Lewy body dementia
Cognitive stimulation activities, reminiscence therapy, and meaningful social engagement improve quality of life and slow functional decline
Carer education, respite care, and support organizations (Alzheimer's Society) are essential to prevent carer burnout
Legal planning (lasting power of attorney, advance care directives) should be completed early while the person retains capacity
dementia.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent evaluation for sudden acute confusion or behavioral change in a person with known dementia — this usually indicates delirium from infection, medication change, or metabolic disturbance rather than dementia progression. Consult a specialist for initial diagnosis, driving cessation advice, and medication management.

immediate_advice: Remove the stinger immediately if present by scraping sideways with a flat card or fingernail — do not squeeze as this injects more venom
Wash the bite site with soap and water and apply a cool compress for 10–15 minutes to reduce swelling and pain
For anaphylaxis (throat tightening, difficulty breathing, collapse): administer epinephrine and call emergency services immediately
quick_solutions: Oral antihistamines (cetirizine, loratadine) reduce itching and local swelling effectively
Topical hydrocortisone 1% cream reduces local inflammation and itch at the bite site
Calamine lotion provides cooling relief for multiple bites causing widespread itching
Insect repellents containing DEET (20–30%) or picaridin applied to skin and clothing are highly effective at preventing bites
insect bite.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for signs of anaphylaxis: throat swelling, difficulty breathing, widespread urticaria with hypotension, or collapse. See a doctor for rapidly spreading redness and warmth (cellulitis), fever with bite, or bites not healing within 2 weeks.
insect bite.specialist_recommendation: General Practitioner / Emergency Physician (for anaphylaxis) / Allergist (for recurrent severe reactions)

immediate_advice: Call emergency services immediately for sustained ventricular tachycardia — this is a cardiac emergency
If the person is pulseless, begin CPR immediately and use a defibrillator (AED) if available
Do not attempt vagal maneuvers for ventricular tachycardia — these are only appropriate for supraventricular tachycardia
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — IV amiodarone or lidocaine, or synchronized DC cardioversion, are used for hemodynamically stable VT; unsynchronized defibrillation for VT without pulse
Identify and correct precipitating factors: hypokalemia, hypomagnesemia, ischemia, prolonged QT interval, or drug toxicity
Implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) is indicated for secondary prevention after survived VT or VF episode
Catheter ablation of the VT focus is recommended for recurrent monomorphic VT in structurally normal or abnormal hearts
paroxysmal ventricular tachycardia.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for palpitations with dizziness, chest pain, presyncope, syncope, or loss of consciousness — ventricular tachycardia can degenerate to ventricular fibrillation and cardiac arrest within seconds.

immediate_advice: Do not rub the eye as this can embed the foreign body further or cause corneal abrasion
Irrigate the eye gently with clean water or sterile saline from the inner corner outward for 15–20 minutes for chemical exposures
Do not attempt to remove an embedded or penetrating foreign body — cover the eye with a shield and seek emergency care immediately
quick_solutions: A superficial, mobile foreign body can be carefully removed by a doctor using a moistened cotton bud or irrigation under slit lamp magnification
After removal, antibiotic eye drops (chloramphenicol) are prescribed to prevent infection
A rust ring left by a metallic foreign body requires removal by an ophthalmologist within 24–48 hours before it causes permanent scarring
For chemical eye injuries (acid or alkali), continuous irrigation for at least 30 minutes and emergency ophthalmological evaluation are mandatory
foreign body in the eye.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency ophthalmological care immediately for suspected penetrating eye injury, high-velocity projectile (metal grinding, power tools), chemical burns, pain persisting after removal attempt, or any visible opacity or laceration on the cornea.

immediate_advice: Keep the affected area clean with gentle soap and water washing twice daily
Apply a clean non-adherent dressing over the wound — change dressings daily or when saturated
Do not squeeze or attempt to drain deep skin infections as this can spread bacteria into deeper tissues
quick_solutions: Topical antibiotics (mupirocin, fusidic acid) are appropriate for small, superficial impetigo or infected wounds
Oral antibiotics (flucloxacillin or cefalexin for Staphylococcal infections; amoxicillin-clavulanate for mixed infections) are required for cellulitis or spreading infection
Mark the border of redness with a pen to monitor whether the infection is spreading despite antibiotics
Fluctuant abscesses require incision and drainage — antibiotics alone are insufficient for established abscess cavities
pyogenic skin infection.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent care for rapidly spreading redness (cellulitis extending significantly), fever, red streaking from the wound (lymphangitis), severe pain out of proportion to appearance (necrotizing fasciitis), facial cellulitis, or infection in a diabetic or immunocompromised patient.
pyogenic skin infection.specialist_recommendation: General Practitioner / Dermatologist / General Surgeon (for abscess drainage)

immediate_advice: Keep the child comfortable with age-appropriate paracetamol or ibuprofen for fever and discomfort
Maintain adequate fluid intake — fever increases insensible fluid losses
Keep the child away from school or daycare until the rash has been assessed and the contagious period has passed
quick_solutions: Most viral exanthems are self-limiting and require only supportive care — antibiotics are not effective for viral illnesses
Calamine lotion or topical antihistamine cream reduces itching from viral rashes
Oral antihistamines (diphenhydramine, chlorpheniramine) provide additional itch relief at night
Ensure the diagnosis is confirmed by a doctor — distinguishing benign viral exanthem from meningococcal rash or drug reaction is critical
viral exanthem.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for a non-blanching rash (does not fade when pressed with a glass — indicates possible meningococcal disease), a child who is very unwell with the rash, rash with stiff neck or severe headache, or rash appearing after starting a new medication.

immediate_advice: Identify and stop the causative agent immediately: medication, food intolerance, dietary indiscretion, or radiation
Maintain adequate hydration with oral rehydration solution — small sips frequently rather than large volumes at once
Rest and avoid solid foods until vomiting has settled, then gradually reintroduce bland, easily digestible foods
quick_solutions: Oral rehydration with electrolyte solutions (ORS) prevents dehydration which is the primary complication
Loperamide (anti-diarrheal) provides symptomatic relief for non-infectious diarrhea in adults — avoid in febrile illness or bloody diarrhea
Dietary exclusion trial (lactose, gluten, specific FODMAPs) helps identify food intolerance as a recurring cause
Anti-emetics (ondansetron, prochlorperazine) are prescribed for significant nausea and vomiting
noninfectious gastroenteritis.when_to_seek_help: See a doctor for symptoms lasting more than 3 days, blood in stool, signs of dehydration, fever above 38.5°C, severe abdominal pain, or in vulnerable patients (young children, elderly, immunocompromised, or pregnant women).

immediate_advice: Seek urgent urological evaluation for complete inability to urinate (acute urinary retention) — catheterization is required immediately
Reduce fluid intake in the evening to minimize nocturia but maintain adequate daytime hydration
Avoid decongestants, antihistamines, and anticholinergics which can precipitate acute urinary retention in men with BPH
quick_solutions: Alpha-blockers (tamsulosin, alfuzosin) rapidly improve urinary flow and reduce symptoms within days
5-alpha reductase inhibitors (finasteride, dutasteride) reduce prostate volume over 3–6 months and are most effective for large prostates
Combination therapy (alpha-blocker plus 5-ARI) is most effective for men with large prostates and significant symptoms
Transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP) or laser prostatectomy is recommended for BPH not responding to medical therapy or with recurrent retention
benign prostatic hyperplasia (bph).when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care for complete inability to urinate or severe urinary retention. Consult a urologist for International Prostate Symptom Score (IPSS) above 8, recurrent urinary tract infections, hematuria, bladder stones, or renal impairment attributed to BPH.

immediate_advice: Ensure adequate lighting for reading and close work to compensate for reduced contrast sensitivity
Use anti-glare coatings on glasses and avoid driving at night if glare is significantly impairing vision
Schedule an ophthalmology appointment for formal visual acuity assessment and cataract grading
quick_solutions: Cataract surgery (phacoemulsification with intraocular lens implant) is the definitive and highly effective treatment — it is one of the most successful surgical procedures performed
Surgery is recommended when the cataract is affecting the patient's ability to perform daily activities, drive, read, or maintain independence
Updated glasses prescription may temporarily improve functional vision in early cataracts
No eye drops or supplements have been proven to slow cataract progression despite commercial claims
cataract.when_to_seek_help: Consult an ophthalmologist when blurred vision, glare, halos around lights, or fading colors are affecting daily activities, driving safety, or quality of life. Seek urgent evaluation for sudden vision loss, monocular diplopia, or a white pupil reflex (leukocoria).

immediate_advice: Lean forward slightly when walking (e.g., use a shopping trolley or walking frame) — spinal flexion opens the spinal canal and relieves neurogenic claudication
Avoid prolonged walking or standing which worsens leg pain and weakness — pace activities with frequent rest breaks
Seek emergency care for any new bladder or bowel dysfunction or saddle anesthesia — these indicate cauda equina syndrome
quick_solutions: Physiotherapy focused on lumbar flexion exercises, core strengthening, and aerobic conditioning significantly reduces symptoms
NSAIDs and neuropathic pain agents (gabapentin, pregabalin) manage the pain and leg symptoms
Epidural corticosteroid injections provide effective medium-term relief of radicular leg symptoms
Surgical decompression (laminectomy or laminotomy) is indicated for severe or progressive neurological deficits or significantly disabling symptoms not responding to conservative management
spinal stenosis.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for new urinary or bowel incontinence, saddle anesthesia (numbness in groin and inner thighs), or rapidly progressive leg weakness — these indicate cauda equina syndrome requiring emergency surgery.

immediate_advice: Call emergency services or go to the emergency department immediately — intestinal obstruction is a surgical emergency
Do not eat or drink anything
Do not take laxatives or enemas as these can worsen the situation in mechanical obstruction
quick_solutions: Seek emergency care immediately — nasogastric decompression, IV fluids, electrolyte correction, and urgent surgical assessment are the standard management
CT abdomen with contrast is the most accurate imaging modality and should be obtained urgently
Surgical intervention (adhesiolysis, bowel resection) is required for complete mechanical obstruction — delay risks bowel ischemia and perforation
Colonic obstruction from cancer may be managed with endoscopic stenting as a bridge to definitive surgery
intestinal obstruction.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for complete absence of bowel movements and flatus, abdominal distension, vomiting (especially feculent vomiting), severe colicky abdominal pain, or any known history of abdominal surgery or cancer with these symptoms.
intestinal obstruction.specialist_recommendation: General Surgeon / Colorectal Surgeon (Emergency evaluation first)

immediate_advice: Maintain adequate hydration if diarrhea or vomiting is present
Avoid foods that worsen symptoms during active flares: high-fiber foods during obstruction, fatty foods in malabsorption, dairy in lactose intolerance
Keep a symptom and food diary to identify patterns and triggers
quick_solutions: Formal diagnosis through colonoscopy, imaging, and laboratory tests is essential before initiating specific treatment
Dietary modifications guided by a gastroenterologist and dietitian address many intestinal conditions effectively
Probiotics may benefit certain intestinal conditions including IBS and post-antibiotic diarrhea
Address nutritional deficiencies (iron, B12, folate, vitamin D) which are common consequences of many intestinal diseases
intestinal disease.when_to_seek_help: Consult a gastroenterologist for persistent altered bowel habit, unexplained weight loss, rectal bleeding, significant abdominal pain, or symptoms lasting more than 4 weeks. Seek emergency care for acute severe pain, peritonism, or signs of bowel obstruction or perforation.

immediate_advice: Rest adequately and maintain high fluid intake to thin mucus secretions
Use paracetamol or ibuprofen for fever, chest discomfort, and myalgia relief
Use a humidifier or steam inhalation to ease airway irritation and facilitate mucus clearance
quick_solutions: Acute bronchitis is most commonly viral — antibiotics are not indicated in the majority of cases and should not be requested
Short-acting bronchodilator (salbutamol) inhaler may provide relief for associated wheezing or breathlessness
Honey (1–2 teaspoons) is as effective as many OTC cough suppressants and is safe for adults and children over 1 year
Avoid cigarette smoke entirely — both active and passive smoke significantly prolongs and worsens acute bronchitis
acute bronchitis.when_to_seek_help: See a doctor for symptoms lasting more than 3 weeks, coughing blood, high fever not settling after 48 hours, breathlessness at rest, symptoms in elderly, immunocompromised, or patients with COPD or asthma, or if pneumonia is suspected.
acute bronchitis.specialist_recommendation: General Practitioner / Pulmonologist (for recurrent or complicated cases)

immediate_advice: Call emergency obstetric services immediately for severe headache, visual disturbances, upper abdominal pain, or sudden significant facial or hand swelling during pregnancy
Do not take any new medications without consulting your obstetrician
Monitor blood pressure at home with a validated device if prescribed and report any reading above 140/90 mmHg immediately
quick_solutions: Seek emergency obstetric care immediately — antihypertensive treatment (labetalol, nifedipine, or methyldopa) is initiated to prevent stroke and eclamptic seizures
Magnesium sulfate is given to prevent eclamptic seizures in severe preeclampsia
Delivery is the definitive cure — timing depends on gestational age, severity of disease, and fetal condition
Low-dose aspirin (75–150mg) started before 16 weeks significantly reduces preeclampsia risk in high-risk women
preeclampsia.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for blood pressure above 160/110 mmHg, severe headache, visual changes (flashing lights, blurred vision), epigastric pain, rapid weight gain with edema, or reduced fetal movement — preeclampsia can progress rapidly to life-threatening eclampsia.
preeclampsia.specialist_recommendation: Obstetrician / Maternal-Fetal Medicine Specialist (Emergency obstetric team first)

immediate_advice: Take prescribed sublingual glyceryl trinitrate (GTN) spray immediately for chest pain — if pain does not resolve after 2 doses 5 minutes apart, call emergency services
Avoid strenuous exertion during acute chest pain episodes
Take all prescribed cardiac medications (aspirin, statin, beta-blocker, ACE inhibitor) consistently without interruption
quick_solutions: Optimal medical therapy (dual antiplatelet therapy, high-intensity statin, ACE inhibitor, beta-blocker) significantly reduces mortality and recurrence
Coronary angiography identifies stenotic lesions amenable to percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI/stenting) or coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG)
Aggressive cardiovascular risk factor modification: smoking cessation, blood pressure control, diabetes management, weight loss, and exercise
Cardiac rehabilitation program significantly reduces mortality, improves functional capacity, and addresses psychological wellbeing after acute events
ischemic heart disease.when_to_seek_help: Call emergency services immediately for chest pain lasting more than 15 minutes not relieved by GTN, associated sweating, jaw or arm pain, breathlessness, or if the character of angina changes significantly (new rest pain, worsening frequency — unstable angina).

immediate_advice: Seek urgent specialist evaluation — any new onset jaundice, unexplained significant weight loss, or new onset diabetes in a middle-aged or older adult warrants prompt investigation
Do not delay referral — early-stage pancreatic cancer has significantly better surgical outcomes
Maintain adequate nutrition — pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy (PERT) is essential if exocrine pancreatic insufficiency is present
quick_solutions: CT pancreas with contrast (pancreatic protocol) and CA 19-9 tumor marker are the primary initial investigations
Surgical resection (Whipple procedure for head tumors, distal pancreatectomy for body/tail tumors) is the only curative treatment — eligible in approximately 15–20% of patients
Palliative chemotherapy (gemcitabine-based regimens or FOLFIRINOX for fit patients) extends survival and maintains quality of life in non-resectable disease
Biliary stenting relieves obstructive jaundice and provides significant symptomatic benefit in unresectable disease
pancreatic cancer.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent evaluation for painless progressive jaundice, pale stools with dark urine, significant unexplained weight loss, persistent upper abdominal or back pain, or new onset diabetes with weight loss in a patient over 50.

immediate_advice: Keep the child calm — crying and agitation worsen laryngeal swelling and stridor significantly
A single dose of oral dexamethasone (0.15–0.6mg/kg) or prednisolone should be given as soon as croup is recognized — this is the most important intervention
Sitting the child upright in a calm, comfortable position improves airflow — do not place the child flat
quick_solutions: Oral dexamethasone is the cornerstone of treatment and dramatically reduces the severity and duration of croup
Nebulized epinephrine (adrenaline) provides rapid temporary relief of severe stridor and is used in emergency settings while awaiting corticosteroid effect
Cool, moist night air (briefly stepping outside) or a cool mist humidifier may provide temporary relief of mild symptoms
Most croup is viral (parainfluenza virus) — antibiotics are not indicated
croup.when_to_seek_help: Seek emergency care immediately for stridor at rest (not just when upset), severe respiratory distress with marked chest retractions, cyanosis, drooling with inability to swallow, altered consciousness, or a child who is not improved or is worsening despite treatment.
croup.specialist_recommendation: Pediatrician / Emergency Physician / Pediatric Intensivist (for severe cases)

immediate_advice: Take NSAIDs (mefenamic acid, ibuprofen, naproxen) starting 1–2 days before menstruation — they reduce menstrual blood loss by 20–40% and also relieve dysmenorrhea
Take tranexamic acid (an antifibrinolytic) during heavy days to reduce blood loss — available without prescription in many countries
Seek urgent care if bleeding is so heavy that it is causing dizziness, faintness, or you are soaking more than one pad or tampon per hour for several consecutive hours
quick_solutions: The levonorgestrel-releasing intrauterine system (Mirena IUS) reduces menstrual blood loss by up to 95% and is the most effective medical treatment
Combined oral contraceptive pill reduces blood loss by 40–50% and regulates cycle timing
Iron supplementation is frequently required as heavy menstrual bleeding causes iron deficiency anemia
Endometrial ablation is a minimally invasive surgical day procedure that reduces or eliminates menstrual bleeding in women who have completed their family
idiopathic excessive menstruation.when_to_seek_help: Consult a gynecologist for bleeding lasting more than 7 days, flooding, passage of clots larger than a 50p coin, anemia symptoms (fatigue, breathlessness, dizziness), or any heavy bleeding that is significantly affecting quality of life.
